en.ace_outfit_description: One of the most technologically advanced bodysuits in the Zone, if not the most advanced. The ACE is a masterpiece of Zone outfit design; not only does it protect from rifle bullets, but it also provides great environmental protection. The suit is also built in a way that allows for a great number of high-tech upgrades that increase the wearer's chance of survival even further, on top of allowing extreme comfort through smart selectively permeable materials. The suit is used by highly experienced and wealthy Mercenaries on high-risk contracts, particularly in areas such as the Red Forest, where they would need to be ready for anything.\n \n
en.af_arhara_globus_descr: Reminds a baby rattle with spines, from where went the name of it... Helps the body to faster restore strength, gives protection against chemical and electrical anomalies. A little radioactive.
en.af_armor_1_descr: Artefact makes it possible to carry a little more things in a backpack. Has no other properties.
en.af_armor_2_descr: Scale in general has beneficial effect on the body. Radioactive.
en.af_armor_3_descr: Armor reduces bleeding of those who wear it. Has no other properties.
en.af_armor_4_descr: This artefact moderately protects against all anomalistic hazards of the Chernobyl Exclusion Zone, but is best against psi waves, which is why it was called so. Radioactive.
en.af_babka_1_descr: Like the Mother's Beads, it stops bleeding, but for a lower radiation dose.
en.af_babka_2_descr: This artifact heals wounds even better, compared to Grandmother's Beads, but the radiation dose increased twice.
en.af_babka_3_descr: Its temporal field is very strong and has a large radius. Raises irradiation.
en.af_blood_green_descr: It looks like the ordinary Stone Blood, but is green. Absolutely not radioactive, emits weak light. Gradually heals body of the owner. Has no other properties.
en.af_caterpillar_descr: Rare artefact that looks like a caterpillar. It was first found in Wild Territory away from any anomalies, so initially it was mistaken for a new creature in the Zone, but later it started to be found in anomalies. Allows to carry more in a backpack, restores strength and slightly removes radiation, but gradually saps health of the owner. Also with this artefact it is better not to approach sources of high temperature.
en.af_dik_1_descr: Anomalous formation from a family of barbed artefacts. Like the Battery, enhances restoration of the body forces pretty well.
en.af_dik_2_descr: Anomalous formation from a family of barbed artefacts. Just like Snowflake, significantly increases restoration of the body forces, but with a lesser radiation dose.
en.af_dik_3_descr: Anomalous formation from a family of barbed artefacts. Makes it possible to carry a little more rounds, restores health and strength. Radioactive.
en.af_dik_4_descr: Anomalous formation from a family of barbed artefacts. Restores health and forces, heals wounds. Radioactive.
en.af_dummy_battery_red_descr: It resembles ordinary Battery, but is red. Not radioactive. Heals scratches and removes radiation quite well.
en.af_dummy_pellicle_red_descr: It resembles the ordinary Pellicle, but is red. Radioactive, emits light. Provides protection against thermal effects.
en.af_dummy_spring_red_descr: It resembles ordinary Spring, but is red. Radioactive. Heals wounds and moderately protects against high temperatures.
en.af_electro_crystal_thorn_descr: Rather common artefact of electrical nature. Helps the organism to quicker restore energy. Radioactive.
en.af_kol_4_descr: This artefact got its name due to the fact that it grants a great strength to a person, however, because of high tension the skin starts to rip and bleeding begins. Radioactive.
en.af_life_heart_descr: Rare artefact, that looks like a real heart. First stalker who found it, almost missed it, taking it for the remains of a less successful fellow. Pretty quickly restores health and strength, but attracts electricity. A little radioactive.
en.af_medusa_green_descr: It resembles the ordinary Jellyfish, but is green. Absolutely not radioactive, emits weak light. It is believed that it appears as a result of the combination of two anomalies. Gradually heals body of the owner and removes radiation, but increases effects that toxins and psi fields have on the organism.
en.af_part_monolit_descr: Extremely rare artefact resembling a pulsating heart. It has a positive effect on the whole body, but a large minus is the strongest amplification of psi waves affecting the owner. Radioactive.
en.af_pudd_1_descr: Anomalous formation from a family of double-disc artefacts. Allows to carry a little more weight in a backpack, gradually restores energy and heals scratches. Radioactive.
en.af_pudd_2_descr: Anomalous formation from a family of double-disc artefacts. Allows to carry a little more weight in a backpack, gradually heals the body. Radioactive.
en.af_pudd_3_descr: Anomalous formation from a family of double-disc artefacts. Restores health, heals wounds and scratches. No negative properties.
en.af_pudd_4_descr: Anomalous formation from a family of double-disc artefacts. When equipped, significantly strengthens muscles of the back, which makes it possible to carry more weight in the backpack. Radioactive.
en.af_simbion_descr: There are rumors that in order to get this artefact, you need to throw certain artefacts into a chemical anomaly. But this is refuted by stalkers who find it just like that in anomalies. Pretty beautiful, helps carry more weight in a backpack and restores health, but increases total energy consumption. Radioactive.
en.af_spirit_1_descr: One of the most valuable artefacts in the Zone. Removes radiation from the body of the owner in a matter of minutes and provides the biggest protection against psi waves. No negative properties.
en.af_spirit_2_descr: One of the most valuable artefacts in the Zone. Quickly heals wounds of the owner and provides the biggest protection against thermal effects. No negative properties.
en.af_spirit_3_descr: One of the most valuable artefacts in the Zone. Quickly restores forces of the owner and provides the biggest protection against electrical impacts. No negative properties.
en.af_spirit_4_descr: One of the most valuable artefacts in the Zone. Quickly restores health of the owner and provides the biggest protection against chemicals. No negative properties.
en.af_vyvert_green_descr: It looks like the ordinary Subwrenched, but is green. Absolutely not radioactive, emits weak light. Gradually heals body of the owner and removes radiation quite well. Has no negative properties.
en.af_water_flower1_descr: This artefact is strangely similar to Water Flower artefact. Among positive properties, should be noted bleeding stoppage, reduced backpack weight and slow radiation removal, but negatively affects digestion.
en.af_water_flower2_descr: Very rare artefact of plant origin. It is valued by stalkers for the help in removing radiation and healing wounds. It also slightly protects against psi fields and absorbs excessive heat. Has no negative properties.
en.af_water_flower_descr: Extraordinary artefact. Probably no one knows its origin. Heal wounds, restores strength and slightly removes radiation, but hunger comes a little faster with it.
en.ammo-11.43x23-fmj_descr:
The .45 calibre full metal jacket round has enjoyed popularity for a hundred years thanks to its ballistic performance and a relatively small propelling charge. As a result, the bullet in this round is relatively slow but highly accurate and provides considerable stopping power.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR1
 -  Flesh damage power: low - medium
en.ammo-11.43x23-hydro_descr:
This unusual .45 ACP calibre round consists of a jacketed soft point bullet with a hollow steel core, possessing both their advantages and disadvantages.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR1
 -  Flesh damage power: high
en.ammo-12.7x55-fmj_descr:
The full metal jacket 12.7x55mm subsonic cartridge is used in the VKS bullpup sniper rifle. The cartridge can carry a projectile weighing between from 59 grams (911 grains) and 76 g (1173 grains), yielding massive amounts of kinetic energy.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR3
 -  Flesh damage power: very high
en.ammo-12x70-buck_descr:
Standard 12 gauge shell filled with 8 mm shot. Highly lethal at close range. Only suitable for use with smoothbore weapons.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: none
 -  Flesh damage power: high
 
SPECIAL:
 
 -  expanding cloud of pellets, short eff. range
en.ammo-12x70-buck_self_descr:
Home-made 12 gauge shell filled with small pieces of metal junk, including used or damaged bullets or even shrapnel. Highly lethal at close range. Only suitable for use with smoothbore weapons.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: none
 -  Flesh damage power: medium - high
 
SPECIAL:
 
 -  expanding cloud of pellets, short eff. range
en.ammo-12x70-raid_descr:
Special 12 gauge shell filled with 8 mm shot.
 
 •  Highest pierced armour class: none
 •  Tissue damage rating: medium - high
 
SPECIAL:
 
 •  expanding cloud of pellets, short eff. range
en.ammo-12x76-bull_descr:
12 gauge custom-made slugs for putting down dinosaurs and mutants. Slug inside is made of lead covered in copper that has steel, dart-like insert inside. Slug is very long and has got big powder load. It really can break your hands with recoil.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR2
 -  Flesh damage power: very high
en.ammo-12x76-dart_descr:
12 gauge dart round, normally used for hunting big game but also suitable for other purposes, including puncturing armour.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR4
 -  Flesh damage power: high-
en.ammo-12x76-zhekan_descr:
This heavy 12 gauge slug round is normally used for hunting big game, but is also good for other purposes. Instead of piercing, this hefty slug simply crushes through armour.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR3
 -  Flesh damage power: high+
en.ammo-338-lapua_descr:
The .338 Lapua Magnum is a specialised rimless bottlenecked centrefire cartridge developed for military long-range sniper rifles. The Afghanistan War and Iraq War made it a combat-proven round with ready and substantial ammunition availability. The .338 Lapua is a dual-purpose anti-personnel and anti-materiel round, but its anti-materiel potential is limited.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR6
 -  Flesh damage power: very high
en.ammo-357-hp_descr:
The .357 HP round is a hollow-point bullet. The purpose of the round is to slow down upon impact, which decreases its penetration, but at the same time causes larger internal tissue damage. 
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR3
 -  Flesh damage power: high
en.ammo-4.6x30-fmj_descr:
The HK 4.6?30mm cartridge is a type of ammunition primarily used in the Heckler & Koch MP7 Personal defense weapon (PDW). It is designed to minimize weight and recoil while increasing penetration of body armor. It features a bottlenecked case and a pointed, steel-core, brass-jacketed bullet. 
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR3 
 -  Flesh damage power: low - medium
en.ammo-5.45x39-ap_descr:
Military 5.45x39 calibre 7N22 armour-piercing round for automatic rifles of Warsaw Pact countries. The bullet contains a hard-alloy armour-piercing penetrator. Effective against body armour.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR5
 -  Flesh damage power: medium
en.ammo-5.45x39-ep_descr:
Military 5.45x39 calibre HP is an hollow point round for automatic rifles of Warsaw Pact countries.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR1
 -  Flesh damage power: high
en.ammo-5.45x39-fmj_descr:
Military 5.45x39 calibre 7N6 was the first standard issue round for automatic rifles of Warsaw Pact countries using the new intermediate cartridge. Provides better accuracy than the 7.62 calibre round thanks to reduced recoil and a 100-metre increase in range.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR3
 -  Flesh damage power: medium
en.ammo-5.56x45-ap_descr:
The latest NATO military 5.56x45 calibre enhanced penetration round, known as M855A1, used by the US armed forces. Increased effectiveness against both body armour and soft tissue.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR5
 -  Flesh damage power: medium
en.ammo-5.56x45-fmj_descr:
A standard-issue NATO 5.56x45 calibre M193 round specially designed for accurate automatic fire. Provides good ballistic characteristics, having been designed on the basis of a hunting round. Well known for being used in the Vietnam war and having great performance against tissue, but limited penetration.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR3
 -  Flesh damage power: medium
en.ammo-5.56x45-ss190_descr:
A standard-issue NATO 5.56x45 calibre round with an hollow point tip.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR1
 -  Flesh damage power: high
en.ammo-5.7x28-ss190_descr:
The FN 5.7x28mm is a high velocity cartridge designed and manufactured by FN Herstal. It is a bottlenecked centrefire cartridge that is somewhat similar to the .22 Hornet or .22 K-Hornet. The 5.7x28mm was developed in conjunction with the FN P90 personal defense weapon (PDW) and FN Five-seven pistol, in response to NATO requests for a replacement for the 9x19mm Parabellum cartridge.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR4
 -  Flesh damage power: medium-
en.ammo-5.7x28-ss195_descr:
The SS195 FN 5.7x28mm is an expansive cartridge designed and manufactured by FN Herstal. It is a bottlenecked centrefire cartridge that is somewhat similar to the .22 Hornet or .22 K-Hornet. 
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR2
 -  Flesh damage power: medium-
en.ammo-50-bmg_descr:
The .50 Browning Machine Gun (12.7?99mm NATO and designated as the 50 Browning by the C.I.P.) is a cartridge developed for the Browning .50 caliber machine gun in the late 1910s, entering official service in 1921. Under STANAG 4383, it is a standard cartridge for NATO forces as well as many non-NATO countries. The cartridge itself has been made in many variants: multiple generations of regular ball, tracer, armor-piercing (AP), incendiary, and saboted sub-caliber rounds. The rounds intended for machine guns are made into a continuous belt using metallic links. 
The .50 BMG cartridge is also used in long-range target and anti-materiel rifles, as well as other .50-caliber machine guns. 
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR6 
 -  Flesh damage power: very high
en.ammo-7.62x25-p_descr:
The 7.62x25mm cartridge is a Russian bottle-necked pistol cartridge widely used in former Soviet satellite states, China and Pakistan among other countries. The cartridge is an enhanced Russian version of the 7.63x25mm Mauser. It has an excellent penetration and can easily defeat lighter ballistic vests as well as kevlar helmets. Although most firearms chambered in this calibre are obsolete and removed from military inventories, the cartridge still sees widespread use because of the large quantity of stored ammunition available. 
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR1
 -  Flesh damage power: low - medium
en.ammo-7.62x25-ps_descr:
The steel core 7.62x25mm cartridge is a Russian bottle-necked pistol cartridge widely used in former Soviet satellite states, China and Pakistan among other countries. It's very effective against unarmoured targets. 
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR2
 -  Flesh damage power: low - medium
en.ammo-7.62x39_ap_descr:
7.62x39 M43 is an armour piercing round produced in China, which is why it's often referred to as "7.62x39 CN". This round features a mild steel core which enhances its penetration capabilities. This Chinese round (as well as all other M43 ammunition) is currently banned for import in the US because US federal law classifies the round as an armour-piercing handgun round. This classification is based on materials and bullet design rather than on empirical ability to penetrate armour.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR6
 -  Flesh damage power: high
en.ammo-7.62x39_fmj_descr:
7.62x39 Full Metal Jacket is one the most commonly used types of ammunition, especially in Africa and in the Middle East. Some nicknamed it the "Round of the Revolution" due to its favored use by both terrorist and criminal organizations. Due to the sheer power of the round most military organizations have turned to using additional steel plates to counter 7.62x39 as there is a high probability of encountering it in combat situations.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR3
 -  Flesh damage power: high
en.ammo-7.62x51_ap_descr:
Military 7.62x51 calibre M61 armor-piercing round with a hardened steel core. It retains high damage while greatly improving performance against body armor.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR6
 -  Flesh damage power: high
en.ammo-7.62x51_fmj_descr:
The 7.62x51 NATO round is a military variant of the .308 round which was used as a standard-issue by the NATO forces until 5.56 ammunition was introduced in the '70s in the Vietnam War. At present, it is most commonly employed by private military contractors all over the world. The 7.62x51 round is a very common machine gun calibre, and can also be used in battle or sniper rifles.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR3
 -  Flesh damage power: high
en.ammo-7.62x54-7n1_descr:
A 7.62x54 calibre sniper round with a steel penetrator specially designed for the SVD. It's steel core is made to pierce light armors. Very common in the zone, these bullets are quite cheap.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR3
 -  Flesh damage power: high
en.ammo-7.62x54-ap_descr:
The 7.62x54mm calibre 7N13 armor-piercing machine gun round. It is a high-powered round and thanks to a hard steel core it possesses quite good penetrative capabilities.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR6
 -  Flesh damage power: high
en.ammo-7.62x54-r_descr:
The 7.62x54 PP is a dated rifle calibre introduced in the Soviet Empire at the turn of the 19th century and was still used by the USSR after World War II. It was originally designed for the Mosin-Nagant standard-issue infantry rifle and is quite common in the Zone due to the popularity of the rifle. The round is relatively cheap (considering it comes in boxes of 50) but has surprisingly good penetrative capabilities, even in comparison to more modern ammo types. It can also be used as a cheaper solution in the PKM machine gun.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR3
 -  Flesh damage power: high
en.ammo-7.62x54_7n14_descr:
The 7.62x54 calibre 7N14 is an armour-piercing cartridge. The bullet has a steel core to penetrate body armour or light barriers, and a boat-tail base to increase accuracy.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR4
 -  Flesh damage power: high
en.ammo-7.92x33_ap_descr:
7.92x57 SA is a round that was used by the British military against armoured targets. It features a hardened steel core which gives it higher piercing capabilities. Used primarily for long range shots, the grain quality of this round is higher than the standard. This cartridge is supported by several sniper rifles.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR4
 -  Flesh damage power: high
en.ammo-7.92x33_fmj_descr:
The 7.92x57 Mauser round was adopted by the German Empire and then the Third Reich. It was one of the most popular cartridges in the period of two world wars. However, nowadays, the round is primarily used for hunting and sports.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR2
 -  Flesh damage power: high
en.ammo-9x18-ap_descr:
RG028 is a cartridge used for its armour penetration capabilities. The goal of the RG028 was to become a counter to body armour; for this reason, it's loaded with a hardened steel core. Unfortunately, the round itself isn't much of an improvement over the standard, it just has a stronger impact than typical 9x18 round.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR3
 -  Flesh damage power: low
en.ammo-9x18-fmj_descr:
A full metal jacket round with a steel penetrator and a lead core under a metal jacket. Provides good stopping power but is ineffective against body armour.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR1
 -  Flesh damage power: low
en.ammo-9x18-pmm_descr:
This round contains a bullet with enhanced stopping power thanks to its hollow point. Ineffective against body armour, but high damage against flesh.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR1
 -  Flesh damage power: medium-
en.ammo-9x19-ap_descr:
This Russian 9x19 calibre bullet comes with a hard steel core and increased pressure. Designed to enhance armour penetrating capabilities of small arms and submachine guns.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR4
 -  Flesh damage power: low - medium
en.ammo-9x19-fmj_descr:
This basic 9x19 calibre round with a jacketed bullet has good characteristics while being simple to produce. The round is popular due to the fact that international conventions prohibit the use of expanding ammunition in combat. Ineffective against body armour.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR2
 -  Flesh damage power: low - medium
en.ammo-9x19-pbp_descr:
This 9x19 calibre jacketed hollow-point bullet with an expanding hollow has high stopping power and low ricochet propensity. Used by police and for self-defense. Ineffective against body armour due to its low penetration potential, but flesh damage. One of the cheapest ammo to efficiently kill mutants in the zone.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR1
 -  Flesh damage power: medium - high
en.ammo-9x39-ap_descr:
The SP-6 is a subsonic armour-piercing round with a jacketed soft point bullet with a hard steel core that protrudes from the casing. Requires a suppressor for stealth firing. Used by special services of Warsaw Pact countries. Effective against light body armour.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR5
 -  Flesh damage power: high
 
SPECIAL:
 
 -  sub-sonic ammunition
 -  greatly increased projectile drop with distance
en.ammo-9x39-pab9_descr:
The SP-5 is a subsonic rifle round with a bimetallic casing. The round contains a steel core with a gap behind it that is filled with lead. Requires a suppressor for stealth firing. Used by special services of Warsaw Pact countries.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR3
 -  Flesh damage power: high
 
SPECIAL:
 
 -  sub-sonic ammunition
 -  greatly increased projectile drop with distance
en.ammo-gauss_cardan_descr:
These lower-capacity cells use capsulated fragments of the "Flash" artefact as their power source. Their lower capacity is due to makeshift production conditions and lack of specialised equipment.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR6
 -  Flesh damage power: very high
 
SPECIAL:
 
 -  technological item
 -  prototype
en.ammo-gauss_descr:
Batteries that use a capsulated fragment of the "Flash" artefact as their power source. Manufactured using hi-tech equipment.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR6
 -  Flesh damage power: very high
 
SPECIAL:
 
 -  technological item
 -  prototype
en.ammo-m209_descr:
40 mm grenade for the M203 underbarrel grenade launcher. This round has a 3.5 metre kill zone. Also suitable for use in the FN F2000 integrated grenade launcher.
 
 -  Blast power: medium
 -  Blast radius: 4.1 m
 -  Fragments' radius: 28 m
 -  Av. number of fragments: 95 (small shrapnels)
 -  Explosion delay: none (explodes on impact)
en.ammo-og-7b_descr:
Fragmentation warhead for anti-personnel warfare used with the RPG-7 grenade launcher. The main purpose of the warhead is to kill enemies in cover outside the range of hand grenades.
 
 -  Blast power: high
 -  Blast radius: 5.6 m
 -  Fragments' radius: 41 m
 -  Av. number of fragments: 153 (small shrapnels)
 -  Explosion delay: none (explodes on impact)
en.ammo-vog-25_descr:
Regular fragmentation grenade for the GP-25 Kostyor underbarrel grenade launcher. This round has a 2.5 metre kill zone. The VOG-25 grenade is also used in the OC-14 Groza complex and RG6 / 6G30 revolver-type grenade launcher.
 
 -  Blast power: medium
 -  Blast radius: 3.6 m
 -  Fragments' radius: 20 m
 -  Av. number of fragments: 81 (small shrapnels)
 -  Explosion delay: none (explodes on impact)
en.ammo_12.7x55_ap_descr:
The armour-piercing 12.7x55mm subsonic cartridge is used in the VKS bullpup sniper rifle. The cartridge can carry a projectile weighing between from 59 grams (911 grains) and 76 g (1173 grains), yielding massive amounts of kinetic energy.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR6
 -  Flesh damage power: very high
en.ammo_20x70_buck_descr:
Standard 20-calibre round filled with 6 mm buckshot. Highly lethal at close range. Only suitable for use with smoothbore weapons
 
 •  Highest pierced armour class: none
 •  Tissue damage rating: medium - high
 
SPECIAL:
 
 •  expanding cloud of pellets, short eff. range
en.ammo_23_eco_descr:
A special cartridge designed for the needs of the scientific staff located in the Zone. Depending on the task, it is filled with different types of solutions. Requires very careful handling. How to use: very slowly put into the weapon.
 
 •  Highest pierced armour class: none
 •  Tissue damage rating: none
 
SPECIAL:
 
 •  Using a thin wolfram needle, it pierces the skin of the mutants to inject the solution.
en.ammo_23x75_barrikada_descr: A cartridge with a steel pointed bullet. It is used to forcibly stop vehicles by damaging them. Application range up to 100 meters. It is used for firing from the KS-23 carbine.\n
en.ammo_23x75_shrapnel_descr: Cartridge with buckshot charge. Application range up to 25 meters. It is used for firing from the KS-23 carbine. Due to the fact that the barrel has rifling in the KS-23 carbine, the buckshot flies over a large area, which allows you to shoot offhand, practically without aiming.\n
en.ammo_338_federal_descr:
The .338 Federal is a rifle cartridge based on the .308 Winchester case necked up to .33 caliber. It was created by Federal Cartridge and Sako in 2006 and intended as a big game cartridge with reasonable recoil for lightweight rifles.
 
 •  Highest pierced armour class: IV
 •  Tissue damage rating: very high
en.ammo_9x21_sp10_descr:
Pistol cartridge with a bullet of increased penetration. Designed to destroy manpower protected by personal protective equipment. The nose part of the heat-strengthened steel core protrudes from the shell, due to which a high penetration effect of the bullet is achieved.
 
 -  Armor penetration power: BR4
 -  Flesh damage power: medium
en.ammo_pg_26_descr:
Cumulative antipersonnel projectile for a grenade launcher. With the help of the RPG-26 launcher, it allows you to hit a sheltered enemy and armored vehicles at a distance exceeding the range of throwing a hand grenade.
 
 •  Blast power: high
 •  Blast radius: 4.2 m
 •  Fragments' radius: 36 m
 •  Av. number of fragments: 153 (small shrapnels)
 •  Explosion delay: none (explodes on impact)
en.ammo_vog_30_descr: A VOG-30 fragmentation grenade is designed to destroy enemy manpower and fire weapons located in open areas. An improved version in comparison with analogues, developed in the 1980s, has a more powerful fragmentation effect, as well as autonomous sealing of the propellant charge in the cartridge.\n
en.army_outfit_description: A poor protective outfit contracted by government bureaucrats outsourcing to the lowest bidder. Typically issued to helicopter pilots, tank drivers, rear-echelon personnel, conscripts and enlisted servicemen. Provides adequate protection from small mutants. Although its overall combat protection is better than the leather jackets commonly found on the rookies of other factions, that really isn't saying much.\n \n
en.attackers_pda_desc: This PDA used to belong to the squad leader. Contains a map of the Red Forest with a set of marked locations, an unchecked one among them. Seems the squad was pursuing someone, and that they were very close to finding them. There is also a lot of additional encrypted data; might be valuable.
en.bandit_exo_outfit_description: A stolen military exoskeleton customised by outlaws to attack well-guarded facilities and to plunder well-armed stalkers. More martially inclined aspiring Bandit Lords tend to favour this armour, using it as a stark and unquestionable symbol of their strength and authority over lesser criminals. After all, for one to keep wearing such a coveted suit among murderers and thieves, one must by necessity possess great strength and cunning.\n \n
en.bandit_exolight_outfit_description: The "underwear" of an exoskeleton, sometimes mistakenly known as the "Radsuit". This outfit provides almost all the protection one can expect from a full exoskeleton, but due to being stripped of its servos and batteries, it provides none of the strength enhancement. On the positive side, this means the suit is now easier to maintain and, unlike a full exoskeleton, does not prevent the wearer from sprinting. Both imposing and highly intimidating in appearance, this outfit is a favourite among the Zone's most powerful and pitiless outlaws.\n \n
en.bandit_nbc_outfit_description: A well insulated but otherwise poorly CBRN suit with a kevlar vest. Before starting its service among the Bandits, it was likely reassembled from the tattered remains of some brutally murdered stalker. It comes standard with a GP-5 gas mask using a hose leading to a modern filter. Although clearly not restored with much care for the Zone's specific conditions, it's nonetheless a popular choice among crooks who appreciate efficient weight distribution systems.\n \n
en.bandit_novice_alt_outfit_description: Jackets like this first entered circulation among Bandits thanks to Quartermaster Morgan, a corrupt supplier who formerly belonged to the Duty faction. Offers protection comparable to a standard Military service outfit. Still not great protection in the Zone, especially not when fighting against enraged or vengeful Dutyers.\n \n
en.bandit_novice_outfit_description: A black leather jacket commonly associated with the Zone's bandits and crooks. Mostly worn to intimidate rookies from other factions, it provides very little protection against anything more than a stiff breeze or the occasional flying vodka bottle.\n \n
en.bandit_ps5_outfit_description: Armour like this first entered circulation among Bandits thanks to Quartermaster Morgan, a corrupt supplier who formerly belonged to the Duty faction. Gives up some of its physical protection for greater comfort and defence against anomalies. These suits are a major sore point for the Duty faction, and getting them out of the hands of anarchists, criminals and murderers is an unspoken priority.\n \n
en.bandit_scientific_dark_outfit_descriptio: A SEVA-like suit made virtually from scratch by one of the Bandits' gifted craftsmen. Compared to the looted SEVAs that are brought in and restored, this patchwork outfit boasts excellent protection against thermal, chemical, radiological and psychic threats, but is otherwise pretty poor for a suit of its class. Still, it's stylish and comfy as all hell, allowing it to become something of a hit among the smooth criminals who can afford it.\n \n
en.bandit_scientific_outfit_description: Bandits have never been terribly interested in the science and research of anomalous fields, but, nevertheless, they use protective suits such as these to organise ambushes and attacks on stalkers where they are not expected at all: in anomalous fields and radiation pockets. The suit has a closed breathing system and an external insulation coating, which provides good protection against all kinds of anomalous effects. Due to a lack of any protection from physical impacts, the suit provides very low protection from bullets or shrapnel.\n \n
en.bandit_sun_outfit_description: A Zone-assembled suit, made by local craftsmen as a matter of survival. Its design seems to be modelled on that of a Special Tactical Suit, but bears an especially disturbing resemblance to a Monolith X-18 suit that has had its conventional NBC features stripped down in favour of greater insulation from thermal and electrical threats. Mostly used by Bandits in Dark Valley to clear out the poltergeists that occasionally escape from the local lab.\n \n
en.banditmerc_outfit_description: A light combat suit obtained through shady means to help armour local Bandit gangs. Its design is based on the suit used by the special forces of Western armies. In a twist of dramatic irony, however, it appears whoever supplied these suits was ripping his customers off. This outfit's overall protection is extremely poor for the Zone's conditions and is significantly worse than the Light Combat suits used by the Zone's Mercenaries. Still, it is at least comfortable to wear, and arguably better than what Ukraine's conscripts get. Arguably.\n \n
en.blackbox_mlr_descr: A flight data recorder, or a "black box". Flight recorders are used to record the status of internal systems and crew conversations, and can provide vital information in the event of an accident. In the Zone, stalker and military personnel alike are interested in these devices.
en.chernobog_notes_desc: "The Zone is a miracle. The Masters cleansed us. Soon, we thought, the Zone would cleanse the world. But no. Our small paradise was soon trespassed upon by the flawed people of a sinful world. And, in an act of unthinkable atrocity, the beast named Strelok crippled the valiant Monolith, and entered the Masters' chambers to slay their mortal forms. However, I shall not despair. I cannot let this miracle be consumed by the vice and filth of the unworthy. So we, Sin, must embark upon a quest to fulfil the Masters' wish. We shall wage war upon the violent to bring about eternal peace."
en.chimera_scan_device_desc: A device designed to record nearby electromagnetic waves, this one has been specifically tuned to isolate the neural oscillations of a specific Chimera specimen.
en.cit_doctors_key_desc: A key with 'Hotel Polissya – Room 26' engraved on side.
en.cit_doctors_pda_desc: 'Just some notes about old men's conspiracy theories'.\n\nThere are rumours that the Agroprom Institute was used as a front for an unofficial lab in the years preceding the 2006 year accident. This lab hosted a number of very unusual experiments. The traders have dug up a lot of information about these laboratories. I met with the Barkeep, he says he sent Duty scouts to get into the underground vaults of the Dark Valley, called X-18, but they disappeared. There are another rumours regarding a 'psi-brain' mechanism. The whole neighbourhood in Yantar is under the influence of that piece of trash: it zombified everyone who came within range. There is another mechanism in the Red Forest radar underground, called lab X19, and that is what is likely to be the Brain Scorcher. There is evidence to suggest that the presence of the Brain Scorcher intentionally blocks the road to the North, but no one knows exactly what it is. The Barkeep believes that the Brain Scorcher is man-made, and these mechanisms are not always operating at full strength. He thinks there are periods when their power is lowered to cool down the apparatus. It's time to visit all the underground laboratories in the Zone and find out what kind of secrets they are hiding. Too bad, that I'm too old for this shit. I wish Strelok would be still around, but damn, he disappeared years ago.
en.contact_lost_pda_desc: This PDA used to belong to the leader of the scout team. He had managed to record some information about the enemy forces before his untimely demise.
en.cs_exo_outfit_description: An experimental version of the army exoskeleton smuggled into the Zone from Ukrainian factories before being refurbished by Clear Sky technicians. Generally, suits like these are held in reserve, to be deployed only in times of true crisis. That said, especially trusted comrades and associates of the faction may eventually receive permission to acquire such a suit for their own personal uses, although not without great cost.\n \n
en.cs_exolight_outfit_description: The "underwear" of an exoskeleton, sometimes mistakenly known as the "Radsuit". This outfit provides almost all the protection one can expect from a full exoskeleton, but due to being stripped of its servos and batteries, it provides none of the strength enhancement. On the positive side, this means the suit is now easier to maintain and, unlike a full exoskeleton, does not prevent the wearer from sprinting. These outfits are smuggled in from Ukrainian factories before being refurbished by the faction's head technician.\n \n
en.cs_nbc_outfit_description: A CBRN suit with a kevlar vest, recently refurbished and modified by Clear Sky. Outfits like these are mass-produced in factories outside the Zone for use in long-term military operations and disaster clean-ups all over the world. It comes standard with a GP-5 gas mask using a hose leading to a modern filter. Each NBC suit that comes into Clear Sky's possession is quality-checked by Novikov to ensure it is suitable for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n
en.cs_scientific_outfit_description: An unofficial version of the SEVA scientific suit, fresh off the smuggling route. Typically used by exploratory groups of the Clear Sky faction. Offers good protection from radiation, but is otherwise of mediocre quality for a SEVA suit.\n \n
en.cs_scientific_outfit_good_description: An unofficial version of the SEVA scientific suit that has been extensively improved by Novikov, Clear Sky's resident technician. While a little more flammable than the typical SEVA, and thus a poor choice for thermal anomalies, it provides beyond excellent anomaly protection in all other cases on top of being extremely comfortable to move around in, and as such is in great demand among Clear Sky's more serious researchers.\n \n
en.cs_stalker_outfit_description: Unlike other suits in the CS-1, 2 and 3 series, this body armour was only created after the faction's massive losses at the Chernobyl NPP. It is widely considered to be the first proper successor to the CS-1 suit as it focuses on keeping the wearer safe from anomalous environments over armouring them for battle. Provides adequate physical protection and good environmental resistances.\n \n
en.cs_stalker_proto_exo_outfit_description: A set of CS-2b body armour that has been specially modified to fit with the servos and batteries of an exoskeleton, allowing the wearer to carry significantly more equipment than they could under any other circumstances, but lacking the armour of a proper exosuit. Compared to a full exoskeleton, it provides less protection and has less efficient servos, but is also considerably cheaper to maintain.\n \n
en.csky_heavy_outfit_description: Formerly the standard outfit issued to Clear Sky assault squads, after the faction’s infamous failure against the NPP in 2011 this armour serves as a reminder that even an excellent protective bodysuit cannot guarantee safety on the wrong side of the perimeter. The armour is capable of stopping a pistol bullet, while the wearer is also reasonably protected from anomalies.\n \n
en.csky_light_novice_outfit_description: A light suit worn by Clear Sky's novice researchers. It provides more than adequate protection from the anomalous phenomena in the Swamps, but does little to protect from physical threats or radiological phenomena.\n \n
en.csky_light_outfit_description: A blue bodysuit intended for conducting rapid search operations in areas of low risk - anomalous or otherwise. Not intended for pitched battle or intense firefights, although it'd likely still fare better than the CS-1 in such scenarios.\n \n
en.csky_medium_outfit_description: A heavier but still comfortable outfit worn by members of Clear Sky that provides more than adequate general protection, especially in combat. However, the armour is also highly conductive and susceptible to corrosion.\n \n
en.csky_novice_outfit_description: A soft blue leather anorak worn over a cosy turtleneck sweater, tucked into sturdy jeans and hiking boots. These simple suits are assembled in large quantities by Clear Sky's tailors for use within the faction. Not recommended for use outside the southernmost parts of the Zone.\n \n
en.decryption_radio_desc: This unassuming military-grade radio can intercept and decrypt nearby transmissions.
en.dolg_exo_outfit_description: Excellent high-tech armour. Domestically developed by one of the research institutes, specifically for the Duty faction. That said, this armour is slowly being phased out in favour of cheaper, more practical suits, and the few exosuits in active service tend to be in the hands of Duty's oldest veterans. Consists of a three-part exosuit (tactical helmet, tactical vest and a kevlar environmental jumpsuit) fitted with modern powered servos.\n \n
en.dolg_exolight_outfit_description: The "underwear" of an exoskeleton, sometimes mistakenly known as the "Radsuit". This outfit provides almost all the protection one can expect from a full exoskeleton, but due to being stripped of its servos and batteries, it provides none of the strength enhancement. On the positive side, this means the suit is now easier to maintain and, unlike a full exoskeleton, does not prevent the wearer from sprinting. This suit is typically found in the hands of elite Duty warriors, especially those assigned to guard VIPs or launch exceedingly dangerous raids.\n \n
en.dolg_heavy_outfit_description: Developed by defence research institutes in Kiev, the first iteration of this Duty bodysuit began as a simple old set of Spetsnaz PSZ-9 body armour, modified for the Zone. Its design has since been continuously improved over the years, yet curiously never took on a new designation. These days, this armour's latest design is capable of stopping a rifle bullet, and boasts great protection against the Zone's myriad horrors in general. The main drawbacks are - naturally - both its bulkiness and its price. It is uncommon for armour as prized as this to be issued to any Dutyer who hasn't proven themselves in some significant manner. \n \n
en.dolg_heavy_proto_exo_outfit_description: A PSZ-9d armoured suit that has been specially modified to fit with the servos and batteries of an exoskeleton, allowing the wearer to carry significantly more equipment than they could under any other circumstances. Compared to a full exoskeleton, it is similarly, if not more, protective in addition to being significantly more versatile on account of being able to change its headgear. That said, its servos are of an older mark, and thus are notably less efficient.\n \n
en.dolg_heavy_redline_outfit_description: A variant of the PSZ armour series that sacrifices some anomaly protection for greatly increased safety against explosions and extreme blunt impacts. This suit was a favourite of the late Lieutenant Colonel Shulga, who rarely ventured outside his operational base in Yanov, yet constantly feared assassination at the hands of Freedom. Thus, heavy protection like this fitted his needs perfectly. As fate would have it, he'd instead later be presumed dead after leading a mass assault into Pripyat with his contingent of troops, never to be seen again. It's not uncommon for Duty sympathizers to claim his sacrifice bought the Zone precious time by slowing the Monolith's sudden resurgence, and veteran Dutyers will occasionally wear suits like this to battle in his honour.\n \n
en.dolg_novice_outfit_description: A dark leather anorak worn over a cosy turtleneck sweater, tucked into sturdy jeans and combat boots. Formerly used only as relaxed wear for off-duty Dutyers, these simple suits were never intended for use by those on active service. Desperate times, however, occasionally call for desperate measures.\n \n
en.dolg_outfit_description: This old version of the Duty bodysuit is better suited for recruits who may prefer the familiarity of stalker suits over frontline battle armour. Unlike the PSZ series, the suit provides reasonable protection against anomalies while also being capable of stopping a pistol bullet.\n \n
en.dolg_pantsir_outfit_description: An "in-between" suit that is more protective than Duty's regular PS5 universal protection suit, yet cheaper and easier to produce than a proper set of PSZ-9d body armour. Ideal for skirmishes, it retains the ballistic and thermal protection of a full PSZ suit, but is otherwise inferior under most other circumstances. Still, its relative ease of maintenance and repair is a great boon to Dutyers far away from friendly territory, such as the investigators who venture up into Yanov or beyond. A great suit overall, although it has displayed a high failure rate against electrical accidents, thunderstorms, and poltergeist attacks.\n \n
en.dolg_proto_exo_outfit_description: A PS5-M universal protection suit that has been specially modified to fit with the servos and batteries of an exoskeleton, allowing the wearer to carry significantly more equipment than they could under any other circumstances, but lacking the armour of a proper exosuit. Compared to a full exoskeleton, it provides less protection and has less efficient servos, but is also considerably cheaper to maintain.\n \n
en.dolg_red_outfit_description: A somewhat heavier variant of the PS5-M universal protection suit, specifically designed for Dutyers intending to hunt mutants. A small amount of ballistic protection is sacrificed in favour of much better protection against common mutant attacks, along with greater protection from the environments such mutants are likely to be found in. Compares well even to modern Duty suits.\n \n
en.dolg_scientific_outfit_description: A wonderful protective suit for the Zone, mostly appreciated because it combines excellent anomaly protection with more than adequate bullet-proofing qualities. It includes the PS5-M suit, a closed cycle breathing system and an integrated system of anomalous field suppression. Provides greater insulation and protection from blunt trauma than a standard SEVA suit with negligible resulting downsides. \n \n
en.dolg_scientific_red_outfit_description: A variant of the Duty scientific suit that has been geared more towards hunting mutants over studying anomalies. Its light weight helps the wearer avoid feeling overburdened, a massive boon against Burers. Decent protection against blunt trauma and fire is naturally helpful against poltergeists. Increased psychic resistance is a must-have for many of the most insidious creatures within the Zone. However, its anti-ballistic/ laceration protection is still roughly what you'd expect from a SEVA, and as such it's not the best choice when fighting bloodsuckers, chimeras or pseudogiants.\n \n
en.dolg_scientific_wood_outfit_description: As a token of goodwill (and to help cover their research costs), Clear Sky occasionally makes coveted "pathfinder" suits on commission for use among the Duty faction. Altered according to Duty's needs, this suit has a few differences compared to the CS-4a pathfinder. Namely, radiological protection atrophies for a focus on defence from chemical weapons, electrical insulation is neglected for thermal retardation, and protection from blunt trauma is lowered in favour of greater safety from lacerations. Overall, an excellent and comfortable outfit for Duty's more curious members.\n \n
en.dolg_scout_outfit_description: An old Duty suit that was developed alongside the PS5-M universal protection outfit. This armour was intended to protect Duty scouts in the early days of the Zone's existence. These days, however, the only parts of the Zone left to be explored are far too dangerous for a light bodysuit like this. As such, these suits are mostly retired now, kept in reserve only to outfit Duty recruits in desperate circumstances.\n \n
en.dolg_specops_red_outfit_description: This suit is a set of former Spetsnaz body armour, bolstered with more substantial protection for one's vital organs. An excellent combat bodysuit, although it has an infamous failure rate in areas of extreme anomalous activity. Despite this, Duty's veterans do not shun this suit, claiming you don't need much protection from anomalies once you know how to spot them.\n \n
en.dolg_voin_outfit_description: A modern, updated variant of the original PS5-M universal protection bodysuit. Is more than adequate for all of the Zone's conditions as well as being markedly comfortable for a Duty suit. Originally intended to replace its predecessor entirely, its higher production cost put this goal on hold in the face of Duty's multitude of recent losses.\n \n
en.drekavac_notes_desc: "Finally, after several years of arduous preparation, we can continue the Masters' plan. It's regrettable that so many people don't believe us, but sometimes words aren't enough. They're blinded by base human desires. If they insist on continuing their violent ways, we'll treat them with violence. Maybe then they will understand. The seed of Vyraj is budding, and I'm here to spread the paradise further and further beyond. I am the tip of the spear."
en.drx_agr_u_documents_desc: A bunch of notes, pictures and a single marked map regarding Agroprom. Probably written by Strelok himself while being around.
en.drx_jup_u_documents_desc: These documents refer to old records of transporting mechanical equipment and tools to the factory in Jupiter. It also includes geometric schemes for the tunnels toward the eastern side.
en.drx_sl_quest_item_1_desc:
Heavily encrypted documents. Most of it is beyond one's ability to crack with what's available, although the introductory page looks like it might be possible. But of course, doing that would be beyond one's paygrade... the job is merely to hand these notes in, not to play around at decoding them.
 
"Jxu Pedu ev Qbyudqjyed - Jxu Hebu ev Ucyiiyedi yd Ckjqdj Hufhetksjyed

Cekbqwui mxe ceij huiucrbut dehcqb xkcqdi unxyryjut jxu qrybyjo je secckdysqju rkj hugkyhut iutqjyed qi jxuo ydlqhyqrbo tyifbqout unjhucu fqhqdeyq, tubkiyedi, eh q tuiyhu je uisqfu ydje jxu kdademd.
