cc -2018"
en.drx_sl_quest_item_2_desc: A locked case. Not sure what's inside but it's heavy. Better be worth it lugging this thing around.
en.drx_x16_documents_desc: A set of old papers that refer to experiments done on rats and other animals.
en.drx_x18_documents_desc: Old documents that refer to an experiment related to psy-waves resonance and emitting.
en.drx_x19_documents_desc: A printed transcription of the radio transmission between scientist groups from the institute.
en.drx_x8_documents_desc: The document refers to what seems to be a massive radar installation. Most likely Brain Scorcher structure and operating system.
en.ecolog_exo_outfit_description: An official version of the military exoskeleton, intended for use by the Ecologists' guards. The suit feels almost impervious to small arms, and its advanced servomotors make it an excellent choice for those in entrenched defensive positions, as it greatly aids in the use of heavy defensive weaponry. When out in the field, wearers of this suit are more commonly used to carrying the ludicrous amount of batteries, LLMCs, AAMs and other supplies an accompanying scientist might need when exploring the Zone.\n \n
en.ecolog_proto_exo_outfit_description: An Ecologist armoured bodysuit, designed for assault and support operations in anomalous areas. Despite the fact that the body armour cannot provide the necessary protection a full exoskeleton offers, the suit has optimized combat performance. The exo-frames are capable of providing mechanical support for carrying heavy loads, such as large quantities of LLMCs, AAMs and other scientific supplies, making it a great choice to aid researchers during investigative trips inside the Zone.\n \n
en.ecologist_guard_outfit_description: A basic suit originally intended as spare equipment for Professor Hermann's "Iskra Scientific Research and Investigation Team", it has since become a relatively cheap protective bodysuit for Ecologist guards working all over the Zone. Boasts more than adequate anomaly protection for its price, but will generally struggle to stop more than a pistol bullet. Most guards will spring for a SEVA or SSP-99M when they can. Those who expect to take part in serious combat actions will rather stick to proper Military combat suits.\n \n
en.enc_zone_artifact_af-ameba-mica: Consists of liquid metal of abnormal origin. It is formed after an Ejection, lasts for a short time, then evaporates.
en.enc_zone_artifact_af-ameba-slime: Cheap artifacts in the form of metal scraps and construction debris. It has no useful properties. Only of interest to researchers for radiation measurements.
en.enc_zone_artifact_af-ameba-slug: Cheap artifacts in the form of metal scraps and construction debris. No useful properties. Only of interest to researchers for radiation measurements.
en.enc_zone_artifact_af-blood: Unique Artifact. The result of the interaction between Crystal Barb and Meatgrinder. Rarely appears in clusters of anomalies.
en.enc_zone_artifact_af-cristall-flower: Born in the anomaly "Trumplin". Such an artifact can be found in few areas of the Zone. Inclusions of metallic compounds give a beautiful play of light. Looking at this artifact in the light of a fire at night is wonderfully soothing.
en.enc_zone_artifact_af-dummy-glassbeads: Superartifact of the second level. Appears in clusters of anomalies. Anomalous energy level: very high. Cost: very high. Prevalence: very low.
en.enc_zone_artifact_af-dummy-spring: Superartifact with unstable gravity. A throwable object is "shot" into a living body after hitting the floor or an obstacle. Once hit, it is "shot" into the next living body until it falls or hits a non-living object. The artifact can then be picked up.
en.enc_zone_artifact_af-electra-flash: Big flying fluff, leaves severe burns when it hits the body. It accumulates in lowlands, pits, and hollows. Rises into the air in wind or explosions. It comes out of a buildup of "Stinging Fluff".
en.enc_zone_artifact_af-electra-moonlight: A congenital case of Elektra anomaly activity. Apparently, such a wonderful rounded shape can be obtained by subjecting the anomaly to thermal effects. An expensive artifact.
en.enc_zone_artifact_af-electra-sparkler: Located near anomalies of the Electra type. A fairly common and inexpensive artifact. Nevertheless, it is valued among Zone researchers for its properties.
en.enc_zone_artifact_af-fireball: Artifact of thermal origin. A transparent shimmering sphere.
en.enc_zone_artifact_af-medusa: Elemental artifact of gravitational nature. Has the shape of a torus, which explains its name. There are at least two known varieties of this artifact, the ordinary and the "Glass Bagel".
en.enc_zone_artifact_af-mincer-meat: Elemental artifact of organic nature. A bundle of stiff lichen fibers of a dirty rusty color. It has no useful properties.
en.enc_zone_artifact_af-night-star: A variety of metal junk, which has been lying in gravitational anomalies for a long time, has acquired the ability to create a directed magnetic field of high intensity. This specimen is a fragment of a gear shaft.
en.enc_zone_artifact_af-vyvert: Located near anomalies of the "Funnel" type. When in contact with the body, it spreads directed radiation that increases stamina. This artifact is not difficult to find, so the money for it is small. It allows you to carry an additional 4 kg.
en.exo_dolg_outfit_description: The "underwear" of an exoskeleton, sometimes mistakenly known as the "Radsuit". This outfit provides almost all the protection one can expect from a full exoskeleton, but due to being stripped of its servos and batteries, it provides none of the strength enhancement. On the positive side, this means the suit is now easier to maintain and, unlike a full exoskeleton, does not prevent the wearer from sprinting. This suit is typically found in the hands of elite Duty warriors, especially those assigned to guard VIPs or launch exceedingly dangerous raids.\n \n
en.exo_dolg_red_outfit_description: The "underwear" of an exoskeleton, sometimes mistakenly known as the "Radsuit". This outfit provides almost all the protection one can expect from a full exoskeleton, but due to being stripped of its servos and batteries, it provides none of the strength enhancement. On the positive side, this means the suit is now easier to maintain and, unlike a full exoskeleton, does not prevent the wearer from sprinting. This suit is typically found in the hands of elite Duty huntsmen, who give up anomalous protection in exchange for greater defence against the most dangerous game.\n \n
en.exo_dolg_urban_outfit_description: The "underwear" of an exoskeleton, sometimes mistakenly known as the "Radsuit". This outfit provides almost all the protection one can expect from a full exoskeleton, but due to being stripped of its servos and batteries, it provides none of the strength enhancement. On the positive side, this means the suit is now easier to maintain and, unlike a full exoskeleton, does not prevent the wearer from sprinting. Old and heavy, it's possible that suits like this were used by the Duty faction to raid Pripyat and clash with Freedom back in 2012.\n \n
en.exo_dolg_wood_outfit_description: The "underwear" of an exoskeleton, sometimes mistakenly known as the "Radsuit". This outfit provides almost all the protection one can expect from a full exoskeleton, but due to being stripped of its servos and batteries, it provides none of the strength enhancement. On the positive side, this means the suit is now easier to maintain and, unlike a full exoskeleton, does not prevent the wearer from sprinting. This suit is typically found in the hands of elite Duty members, particularly those conducting operations in dangerous anomalous areas like the Red Forest.\n \n
en.exo_merc_grass_outfit_description: The "underwear" of an exoskeleton, sometimes mistakenly known as the "Radsuit". This outfit provides almost all the protection one can expect from a full exoskeleton, but due to being stripped of its servos and batteries, it provides none of the strength enhancement. On the positive side, this means the suit is now easier to maintain and, unlike a full exoskeleton, does not prevent the wearer from sprinting. An outfit of choice for the captains and bodyguards of Mercenary companies when actively working out in the field, particularly in areas of high thermal energy.\n \n
en.exo_merc_urban_outfit_description: The "underwear" of an exoskeleton, sometimes mistakenly known as the "Radsuit". This outfit provides almost all the protection one can expect from a full exoskeleton, but due to being stripped of its servos and batteries, it provides none of the strength enhancement. On the positive side, this means the suit is now easier to maintain and, unlike a full exoskeleton, does not prevent the wearer from sprinting. An outfit of choice for the captains and bodyguards of Mercenary companies operating in areas like Limansk or Pripyat.\n \n
en.exo_merc_wood_outfit_description: The "underwear" of an exoskeleton, sometimes mistakenly known as the "Radsuit". This outfit provides almost all the protection one can expect from a full exoskeleton, but due to being stripped of its servos and batteries, it provides none of the strength enhancement. On the positive side, this means the suit is now easier to maintain and, unlike a full exoskeleton, does not prevent the wearer from sprinting. An outfit of choice for the captains and bodyguards of Mercenary companies operating in combat zones of highly anomalous thermal activity.\n \n
en.exo_outfit_description: An experimental sample of a military exoskeleton. Was never mass-produced due to extraordinary cost and some design flaws. Despite this, it is in demand due to its ability to take on the weight of all carried equipment, and therefore small batches are made in underground facilities outside Ukraine. Consists of a three-part exosuit (tactical helmet, tactical vest and a kevlar environmental jumpsuit) fitted with modern powered servos.\n \n
en.exo_wood_outfit_description: The "underwear" of an exoskeleton, sometimes mistakenly known as the "Radsuit". This outfit provides almost all the protection one can expect from a full exoskeleton, but due to being stripped of its servos and batteries, it provides none of the strength enhancement. On the positive side, this means the suit is now easier to maintain and, unlike a full exoskeleton, does not prevent the wearer from sprinting. Compared to a standard exosuit, it gives up some combat resistances for greater protection from the environment.\n \n
en.exolight_outfit_description: The "underwear" of an exoskeleton, sometimes mistakenly known as the "Radsuit". This outfit provides almost all the protection one can expect from a full exoskeleton, but due to being stripped of its servos and batteries, it provides none of the strength enhancement. On the positive side, this means the suit is now easier to maintain and, unlike a full exoskeleton, does not prevent the wearer from sprinting. Consists of an integrated tactical helmet, a heavy armoured vest and a reinforced environmental jumpsuit.\n \n
en.freedom_exo_vineleaf_outfit_description: The "underwear" of an exoskeleton, sometimes mistakenly known as the "Radsuit". This outfit provides almost all the protection one can expect from a full exoskeleton, but due to being stripped of its servos and batteries, it provides none of the strength enhancement. On the positive side, this means the suit is now easier to maintain and, unlike a full exoskeleton, does not prevent the wearer from sprinting. Quite light and comfortable for a suit of its class, it is also noticeably less protective against shrapnel or chemical weapons.\n \n
en.freedom_nosorog_outfit_desc: A next generation exoskeleton, consisting of a doubly reinforced exosuit and an armoured Czech-M10 gas mask. This suit is rumoured to have been the brainchild of a now deceased stalker named "Fang", whose designs were later claimed by all sorts of powerful factions across the Zone. If regular exoskeletons allow stalkers to feel like a light vehicle, the Juggernaut almost makes them believe they're a main battle tank, able to withstand incredible punishment while carrying tremendous ordnance. Typically only available to a faction's most valuable members, and even then only during exceptionally dangerous missions.\n \n
en.ghillie_outfit_description: This suit is designed to simulate a ‘heap of foliage.’ Usually this type of suit is worn by snipers and hunters to blend in with the environment and hide themselves from enemies. Ghillie suits are commercially produced, but usually both military personnel and civilians customise and improve them to suit their needs. Despite its high efficiency, it can be very impractical: even in a temperate climate, it becomes hot over time and is very heavy.\n \n
en.greh_armored_camo_outfit_description: A more advanced variant of regular Sin flak armour, typically gifted to the faction's more loyal and devout members. Compared to its more standard counterpart, this suit provides increased protection from anomalies in addition to fortifying the wearer's strength beyond its often already considerable limits.\n \n
en.greh_armored_outfit_description: A comfortable and ergonomic arming suit used to outfit Sin's followers en masse. It consists of a ballistic vest over an environmental bodysuit. Providing passable protection from anomalies and good protection from gunfire, these suits do a good job fortifying the already durable warriors of Sin.\n \n
en.greh_exo_outfit_description: High-tech armour, based on the original experimental military designs. However, as far as anyone can see, its servos are made of alloys and materials unknown to any field of science in any other part of the world. Consists of a three-part exosuit (tactical helmet, tactical vest and an anti-ballistic environmental jumpsuit) fitted with modern, if somewhat alien, powered servos. Wearing this suit creates an unnatural feeling of bliss and calm within the wearer, although whether this sense of security is genuine or not remains to be seen.\n \n
en.greh_ps5_outfit_description: This outfit's design is based on the PS5-M universal protection suit. It is unknown how this armour has come into the possession of Sin. Nevertheless, Duty would not be happy to know their armour designs are being appropriated by Zone-worshipping fanatics, and would likely make the recovery of these suits an unspoken priority. The suit itself is of good quality, and more than adequate for the Zone's conditions.\n \n
en.greh_trenchcoat_outfit_description: A long, thick, bloodstained overcoat worn over a butchered scientist suit. Although the closed cycle system and several other features seem to have been stripped off for whatever reason, the end result still compares favourably to many basic stalker suits.\n \n
en.helm_ach7_descr: The ACH-7 (Advanced Compound Headgear, 7 Parts) is a more modern version of the surplus steel helmet. It is marginally better at stopping shrapnel. Its main draw, however, is its surprising resistance to psychic attacks, along with its unexpectedly low cost. That said, its lack of a respiratory device makes it an extremely poor choice for the Zone's conditions overall, and is typically only purchased by rookies brave enough to investigate psy-anomalies so early in their stalking careers.\n \n
en.helm_ach7ex_descr: The ACH-7EX is an "Extended" version of the original ACH-7 headgear set. The inclusion of a respiratory system alone makes this model a major improvement over its predecessor. Boasting chemical and radiological protection somewhere between that of a GP-5 and a PBF kit, along with respectable physical protection makes this headgear an appealing choice for many stalkers who are looking for a good but economical balance between anomalous and combat protection.\n \n
en.helm_m50_descr: An M50 gas mask tweaked for better functionality in the Zone. Although some of its protective characteristics are actually slightly worse as a result, it makes up for this in a much-needed boost to protection from both psychic and radioactive phenomena. Overall, this mask is considered an improvement over the M40. Unlike the M40, however, this mask is bought directly by private military companies for the express purpose of supplying the Zone's Mercenaries. \n \n
en.helm_ppm88_descr: A Russian gas mask well suited for CBRN ecological hazards. In the Zone, it can be considered a functional and spiritual successor to the GP-5, possessing comparable overall characteristics alongside significant improvements in protection versus chemical and radiological threats. A good option that most stalkers can use throughout their careers, provided they don't see too much combat or do anything similarly foolish.\n \n
en.helm_ranger_descr: Using the GP-10 as its model, this gas mask was designed primarily to protect the wearer from controllers and poltergeists. The success rate is somewhat mixed, but this model remains an excellent option for those who need protection from psychic, thermal, and shrapnel attacks.\n \n
en.helm_spartan_descr: A helmet commissioned by extremely influential stalkers, based off the Apocalypse helm designs formerly used by "Final Day". Compared to the Apocalypse, however, this helmet is not only lighter, but more protective across the board. It compares highly favourably to modern combat helmets such as the "sphere" series in addition to boasting amazing protection against anomalies. A popular choice among stalkers who can get hold of it.\n \n
en.hybrid_outfit_description: A bit of an awkward duckling, this outfit was created by one of Kiev's defence research institutes for the purpose of guarding researchers in the Zone. It is clear the suit was created on a budget, however, as the ballistic armour woven into the outfit simultaneously makes it weaker against anomalous phenomena, while still providing less protection in a firefight than a Beryllium vest. As such, none of these suits were ever officially commissioned by the Ukrainian government, although a few nonetheless managed to find their way into the Zone through shady means. A popular choice for stalkers who want decent general protection yet cannot spring for something better.\n \n
en.isg_exo_outfit_description: The official version of the UNISG exoskeleton. Released in limited quantities by special order of the United Nations. While it still possesses many of the design flaws of other exoskeletons, it's nonetheless seen as a great alternative to light, command and scout vehicles, which would have great trouble navigating the Zone anyway, and thus is deployed in their stead.\n \n
en.isg_exolight_outfit_description: The "underwear" of an exoskeleton, sometimes mistakenly known as the "Radsuit". This outfit provides almost all the protection one can expect from a full exoskeleton, but due to being stripped of its servos and batteries, it provides none of the strength enhancement. On the positive side, this means the suit is now easier to maintain and, unlike a full exoskeleton, does not prevent the wearer from sprinting. The UN reserves these suits for only its most successful agents and talented commanders.\n \n
en.isg_lcs_camo_outfit_description: A modified "Light Combat Suit" model adapted for use in the Zone by UNISG troops. Its design is based on the suit used by the special forces of Western armies. Despite its light weight and general comfort, this outfit demonstrates more than adequate protection against anomalies and during gunfights against lightly armed opponents. Sadly, it is not of much help against shrapnel, bludgeoning or stab wounds.\n \n
en.isg_lcs_outfit_description: A "Light Combat Suit" model adapted for use by UNISG troops. Its design is based on the one used by the special forces of Western armies. Compared to the Mercenary variant, it has better anomaly protection and worse combat protection. Comes with built-in body armour and armour attachment modules, but is mediocre overall outside the most basic of operations within the Zone.\n \n
en.isg_lcs_urban_outfit_description: A modified "Light Combat Suit" model adapted for use in battle by UNISG troops. Its design is based on the suit used by the special forces of Western armies. Compares favourably in combat compared to many other outfits in its class, but still falls short of what a Spetsnaz operator would be wearing. Not recommended for use in areas of high anomalous or radiological activity.\n \n
en.isg_merc_outfit_description: An improvement over the original Mk-1 design, this outfit is much better suited for the Zone's conditions. Its overall protection against anomalies is more than adequate, and particularly great protection is afforded against chemical, toxic or thermal hazards. These improvements were commissioned by UNISG based on the limited data that could be recovered from their less fortunate predecessors. Still, wearers are encouraged to avoid contact with local stalkers as often as possible.\n \n
en.isg_outfit_description: Based on a fairly standard suit design used by Western special forces, outfits like this are common to many militaries throughout the world. This is the bare minimum a UNISG operative can expect to be fitted with. Compared to even a basic Mercenary LC suit, this body armour is both heavier and less protective and as such can be considered barely adequate for the Zone's conditions. Still, it is a massive step up from the army uniforms found on Ukrainian conscripts.\n \n
en.isg_proto_exo_outfit_description: A UN-branded special tactical suit that has been specially modified to fit with the servos and batteries of an exoskeleton, allowing the wearer to carry significantly more equipment than they could under any other circumstances, but lacking the armour of a proper exosuit. Compared to a full exoskeleton, it provides less protection and has less efficient servos, but is also considerably cheaper to maintain.\n \n
en.isg_scientific_outfit_description: Commissioned by UNISG from one of Kiev's defence research institutes especially for research in the Zone. The closed system of breathing and special treatment materials provide excellent protection against harmful radiation and anomalous effects, even compared to other SEVA suits. However, the built-in body armour isn’t the best and can break somewhat easily, so is not suitable for active hostilities.\n \n
en.item_decoder_desc: A powerful electronic lock-pick. Strangely enough, it does not seem to be an all-purpose thieves' instrument. Rather, it seems to have been created with a specific electronic lock in mind and can't be used to override anything else.
en.item_doc_jup_ug_desc: The notebook contains a detailed description of an experiment assessing a psi-field's effect on an object in another hemisphere. A group of scientists in the Caribbean received the signal, but it was distorted. The signal was altered in a strange way, as though corrected by someone. A possible explanation is that the noosphere theory is correct and the changes were made by the noosphere.
en.item_doc_mon_con_name_desc: Documents which describe the operating mechanism and main technical characteristics of a more advanced electromagnetic device.\nThere is a note inside the documents which reads: 'Documents must be returned to X8, the central laboratory. I will be busy, so deliver them yourself. — N. Lebedev'
en.item_doc_x10_desc: The documents describe the technical characteristics of an antenna and how they deviate from their planned designs. There is a note on the reverse side which indicates that these documents were prepared in lab X19.
en.item_doc_x16_desc: The documents refer to one of the side discoveries of bio-research, specifically the discovery that living creatures are able to generate directed psy-emissions. A biological field emitter, essentially a giant brain, was grown as part of the research conducted in lab X16.
en.item_doc_x18_desc: The report details the study of biological samples sent from lab X-18. The samples were subjected to informational field effects of varying intensities. The report meticulously describes the mutation of living beings following exposure to emissions.
en.item_doc_x8_desc: The document refers to the transfer of the research team and all materials to another laboratory far in the north, which will be used to research matters relating to the influence of the noosphere. To enter the lab, use the date of the accident as the code (ddmmyyyy).\n \nThe research team comprised: N.A. Lebedev, E.F. Kalancha and V.I. Suslov.
en.item_doc_x8_ll_desc:
These documents briefly touch on the results of extensive experiments in psy-research conducted by an unknown group. Most notably, the results seem to confirm the existence of a non-contact field of psychic energy which envelops the Earth.
 
Also contained is the brief description of sophisticated machinery that could potentially influence said field in a controlled fashion.
 
The bulk of these documents, however, are simply a long list of human test subjects who were exposed to psy-emissions of various frequencies and intensities. Many were noted to have seemingly developed superhuman endurance, intellect or telepathic abilities during the testing. Some are recorded to have later died, others were transferred, and many were left completely unaccounted for, as if they disappeared or escaped.
 
Scribbled along one of the margins is a cryptic note: "Already we have perverted the human form far beyond recognition for the sake of our vision! How much longer will it be before our hubris and sin catches up to us?". Strelok will definitely be interested in this.
en.item_monolith_shard_desc: This object was, according to legend, split from the Wish Granter itself. Displays properties not unlike an artefact. It crackles with unnerving energy, and may boast insidious powers that aren't immediately apparent.
en.item_pda_map_mlr_descr: A PDA containing possible information on routes between different areas.
en.jup_b1_half_artifact2_descr: Rare artefact. Protects from psi fields and slightly from electricity. It also has a field that lightens objects around it a little. No negative properties.
en.jupiter_documents_desc: Presumably the last documents which contain secret information left in the factory. Better not let them fall into the hands of foreign agents.
en.light_dolg_outfit_description: An old IBA suit imported from the West and modified for the Zone's conditions. Uncomfortably heavy both to wear and to carry, the Duty variant seems to have received extensive upgrades for protection against chemical weapons. It is unlikely that Duty order these suits from the outside world themselves, however. Rather, it is widely believed that they are simply refurbished and restored after being salvaged off the bodies of vanquished anarchists.\n \n
en.light_freedom_outfit_description: Rumoured to be supplied to the Freedom faction directly from an unknown source, this suit is based on the somewhat outdated IBA system, but modified for greater comfort when operating in the Zone's conditions. A respectable suit of body armour, its intended use in the Zone may well be to properly protect skirmish forces expected to assault lightly entrenched enemy positions.\n \n
en.light_isg_outfit_description: A strong but somewhat outdated combat suit modified to additionally give adequate anomalous protection to the wearer. This suit is the bare minimum that one should wear in situations where conflict with armed SSU forces seems inevitable. Also not a bad choice for UN scouts who intend to conduct light raids into the Zone's myriad laboratories.\n \n
en.light_loner_outfit_description: An old IBA suit imported from the West and modified for the Zone's conditions. It's a little heavy, but provides fair anomaly protection along with good anti-ballistic characteristics. Sometimes worn by stalkers hired as auxiliaries to help fight wars between factions, this suit significantly improves one's chance of surviving a skirmish compared to regular stalker gear.\n \n
en.light_merc_outfit_description: A well-kept Interceptor Anomalous Body Armour System sold directly to the Mercenaries from various Western sources. This suit, while a little uncomfortable, is overall better than the standard model circulating around the Zone. It's intended to allow Mercenaries to fulfill contracts that call for light assaults on fortified enemy positions within the Zone. \n \n
en.light_monolit_outfit_description: An Interceptor Anomalous Body Armour System that has somehow ended up in the hands of the Monolith. Compared to the standard model, this suit is slightly more ergonomic and boasts greater protection against psychic interference. These vests usually enter the faction by way of hapless stalkers getting stuck trying to cross the Barrier. More often than not, the next time they're seen by the Zone at large is during the Monolith's next invasion into the Warehouses.\n \n
en.light_voen_outfit_description: An Interceptor Anomalous Body Armour System that used to belong to a Military Stalker. Compared to the standard model, this suit is more comfortable to wear and features overall superior anomaly protection. Not normally issued to conscripts or Spetsnaz operators, Military Stalkers who wish to use this suit generally have to order it themselves.\n \n
en.lttz_ll_wpn_gauss_quest_descr: Extremely powerful and highly accurate electromagnetic accelerator gun. Obtains energy from electric-type artefacts. Can penetrate even the best armour. Someone apparently abandoned this hand-held version on the shooting range of its much bigger prototype.
en.lx8_history_2_documents_desc: This log documents the results of an experiment happened long years ago regarding psy-waves applications.
en.lx8_history_3_documents_desc: This log documents the results of a biological experiment.
en.lx8_history_documents_desc: This log documents the results of an experiment happened long years ago regarding new types of psy-emitters.
en.merc_combat_scientific_outfit_descriptio: As a token of goodwill (and to help cover their research costs), Clear Sky occasionally makes coveted "pathfinder" suits on commission for use among the Zone's Mercenaries. Compared to the average SEVA suit, this model is superior across the board. Compared to the CS-4a pathfinder, this suit has poorer protection against radiation, psy-fields and blunt trauma, but is lighter with generally better protection overall.\n \n
en.merc_coyote_outfit_description: A medium tactical suit, nicknamed the "Coyote". Widely considered the successor of the LTS-J body suit, this outfit boasts better physical protection on top of an even better weight distribution system. Naturally, its price is higher than that of its predecessor, but this won't stop most Mercs from springing for it when they are able.\n \n
en.merc_exo_outfit_description: An experimental sample of a military exoskeleton of western production for equipping Mercenaries and other armed groups. While not always the most practical suit to maintain in the Zone, it simultaneously allows the wearer to be both heavily armed and extremely well armoured, with enough strength to carry weeks worth of supplies. Most often worn as a morale booster and highly protective utility suit by veteran Mercenary captains expected to lead their men through hellish conditions. Consists of a three-part exosuit (tactical helmet, tactical vest and a kevlar environmental jumpsuit) fitted with modern powered servos.\n \n
en.merc_exolight_outfit_description: The "underwear" of an exoskeleton, sometimes mistakenly known as the "Radsuit". This outfit provides almost all the protection one can expect from a full exoskeleton, but due to being stripped of its servos and batteries, it provides none of the strength enhancement. On the positive side, this means the suit is now easier to maintain and, unlike a full exoskeleton, does not prevent the wearer from sprinting. An outfit of choice for the captains and bodyguards of Mercenary companies when actively working out in the field.\n \n
en.merc_fighter_outfit_description: Sometimes bitterly referred to as the "Fighter" by sarcastic Mercenaries, this suit is a variation of the standard LC outfit that has sacrificed a great deal of protection for increased mobility, better thermal protection and a belt slot for artefacts. It can perform surprisingly well in operations where stealth and speed are imperative, and thus finds limited used among Merc scouts and hitmen who operate in the less irradiated parts of the Zone.\n \n
en.merc_jackal_outfit_description: A light tactical suit, nicknamed "Jackal" after the now dead Merc leader who was assigned to crack open a laboratory named X-8 over six years ago. This outfit provides more-or-less adequate protection from the Zone's dangers, but what sets it apart is how marvellously comfortable it is on top of possessing a fairly sophisticated weight distribution system. This, on top of a relatively affordable price, makes it a favourite among slightly more experienced Mercenaries.\n \n
en.merc_nighthunter_outfit_description: Sometimes nicknamed the "Nighthunter", this outfit is a slightly heavy special tactical suit, used by Mercenaries in both nocturnal and urban combat scenarios. In terms of design and performance, it strikes a good balance between typical Spetsnaz armour and a common stalker suit. It possesses more than adequate protection for skirmishes, but is generally considered mediocre by those who prefer artefact hunting contracts over fulfilling hit jobs. \n \n
en.merc_outfit_description: The "Light Combat Suit" is one of the most common outfits used by the Zone's Mercenaries. Its design is based on the suit used by the special forces of Western armies. Due to a special treatment of the fabric, the armour has a strengthened stability during the physical movement of its plates. Adequate for basic operations, but quite poor in quality compared to standard Spetsnaz gear, and was quite obviously not designed with the existence of anomalies in mind.\n \n
en.merc_scientific_armored_outfit_descripti: A variant of the SEVA bodysuit that was commissioned by Mercenaries. Some of its environmental protection systems were stripped down to make room for armoured plates that wouldn't impede mobility. Used by veteran Mercs who are expected to guide researchers and artefact hunters in the more dangerous parts of the Zone. Has good physical, thermal and chemical protection for a SEVA, but there definitely are better choices when diving into other anomaly types.\n \n
en.merc_scientific_outfit_description: A variant of the SEVA bodysuit that was commissioned by Mercenaries. A reasonable amount of physical protection was sacrificed for the sake of greater mobility. Used by veteran Mercs hunting artefacts near the centre of the Zone, often accompanied by a group of trusted guards who are more prepared for combat than the wearer. Has excellent anomaly protection overall, even for a SEVA, but suffers a little in thermal and psy anomalies.\n \n
en.merc_scout_outfit_description: Nicknamed the "Scout", this outfit is, essentially, an LC-F suit modified to fill a more specialized niche. While a little heavier and less protective in combat than an LC-F, it is still a very comfortable suit. A cheap source of environmental protection, this suit's modifications make it a good choice for Mercenaries who want to explore the Zone's anomalies on a budget.\n \n
en.merc_sun_outfit_description: A Zone-assembled suit, made by local craftsmen as a matter of survival. Clearly, the resident Mercenaries have taken a leaf out of the Free Stalkers' book, and have begun to locally produce some of their own outfits. Shows signs of inspiration from the Monolith X-18 suit - an outfit the Mercs no doubt try to salvage from their costly skirmishes in Limansk. Overall of good quality, most Mercenaries find it more than adequate for mid-level operations south of Radar.\n \n
en.merc_sunset_outfit_description: A Mercenary Light Combat Suit modified for better use in the Zone. Possessing overall improvements over the standard LC suit across the board, it is generally considered more than adequate for light combat engagements, but a little uncomfortable to use in anomalous areas. Still, this suit is a genuine improvement all around for most newer Mercenaries, and - for general use - is not a bad alternative to the faction's special tactical suits.\n \n
en.merc_wolven_outfit_description: Sometimes nicknamed the "Wolven", this outfit is a comfortable special tactical suit, used by Mercenaries in both exploratory expeditions and stealth operations. This suit possesses good anomaly protection for its class, allowing the wearer to move through - or hide in - dangerous anomalous fields in relative safety. Naturally useful for artefact hunting, this protection also allows large teams of Mercenaries to set up ambushes in unexpected locations, allowing them to take down targets considered too tough for more orthodox methods.\n \n
en.military_exo_outfit_description: The official version of a military exoskeleton. Released in limited quantities by special order to the Military Stalkers, who must pay for it themselves. Designed to carry heavy weapons like grenade launchers and machine guns, along with enough ammunition, food and meds to keep the unit supplied for long periods of time. Occasionally fielded in lieu of light vehicles.\n \n
en.military_exolight_outfit_description: The "underwear" of an exoskeleton, sometimes mistakenly known as the "Radsuit". This outfit provides almost all the protection one can expect from a full exoskeleton, but due to being stripped of its servos and batteries, it provides none of the strength enhancement. On the positive side, this means the suit is now easier to maintain and, unlike a full exoskeleton, does not prevent the wearer from sprinting. Considered highly experimental by the Ukrainian government, these suits are never issued to any unit or individual. Thus soldiers - most often Military stalkers and the occasional old veterans - who wish to use such armour must always find and pay for it through their own means.\n \n
en.military_outfit_description: An excellent protective suit for the Zone. This heavy military-grade armour is designed for major battle operations in anomalous areas. Comprises a PSZ-12p heavy military armoured suit, built-in compensation suit, and an anomalous protection bodysuit. The armour provides excellent protection against bullet and fragmentation damage, without greatly reducing the wearer’s mobility. Although originally intended for the sole use of Military stalkers and experienced Spetsnaz operators, this armour occasionally finds its way into factions outside of the Military through unknown means.\n \n
en.military_proto_exo_outfit_description: A Skat-9 armoured suit that has been specially modified to fit with the servos and batteries of an exoskeleton, allowing the wearer to carry significantly more equipment than they could under any other circumstances. Compared to a full exoskeleton, it is similarly, if not more, protective in addition to being significantly more versatile on account of being able to change its headgear. That said, its servos are of an older mark, and thus are notably less efficient.\n \n
en.monolith_exo_outfit_description: High-tech armour, made on the basis of a military exoskeleton. However, study samples have revealed that its servos are made of alloys and materials unknown to any field of science in any other part of the world. Consists of a three-part exosuit (tactical helmet, tactical vest and an anti-ballistic environmental jumpsuit) fitted with modern, if somewhat alien, powered servos. Wearing this suit feels like being buried under the concrete of the Sarcophagus itself - terrifyingly claustrophobic for most stalkers, but eerily comforting for true servants of the Monolith. \n \n
en.monolith_exolight_outfit_description: The "underwear" of an exoskeleton, sometimes mistakenly known as the "Radsuit". This outfit provides almost all the protection one can expect from a full exoskeleton, but due to being stripped of its servos and batteries, it provides none of the strength enhancement. On the positive side, this means the suit is now easier to maintain and, unlike a full exoskeleton, does not prevent the wearer from sprinting. A favourite among the Monolith's battle preachers and tactical masters, who claim the confines of the suit to be as comforting as the Sarcophagus itself.\n \n
en.monolith_nbc_outfit_description: An old CBRN suit with a kevlar vest, most commonly associated with the fanatical zealots who defend the centre of the Zone from other stalkers. These suits were first used by military personnel after the 1986 accident in order to aid in the cleanup process. Shows obvious signs of immense age and irreparable wear. How so many of these suits were left behind, and how they eventually found their way into the hands of the Monolith, is a mystery. Comes with a refurbished integrated GP-5 gas mask.\n \n
en.monolith_outfit_description: A mysterious outfit associated with the Monolith faction. It consists of an environmental bodysuit that provides more than adequate chemical and radiological protection, underneath a ballistic vest capable of stopping weak pistol rounds. Its anomaly protection, however, is subpar. Previously of unknown origin, scores of these suits were found in containers and lockers underneath the X18 facility in Dark Valley. Although the stalkers who found this cache have since all mysteriously disappeared, this discovery has nonetheless launched thousands of conspiracy theories among stalkers of other factions, the premise of many claiming that these suits predate the creation of the Zone itself.\n \n
en.monolith_proto_exo_outfit_description: An armour made by either Monolithians or an unknown manufacturer. It has been specially modified to fit with the servos and batteries of an exoskeleton, allowing the wearer to carry significantly more equipment than they could under any other circumstances, but lacking the armour of a proper exosuit. Compared to a full exoskeleton, it provides less protection and has less efficient servos, but is also considerably cheaper to maintain.\n \n
en.monolith_scientific_light_outfit_descrip: A SEVA-like suit made virtually from scratch by an unknown craftsman. Compared to an ordinary Monolith scientific suit, this outfit is lighter and provides better protection from both thermal and chemical burns. Other than that, however, it's outright worse. It is unknown what role this suit fills within the Monolith faction as a whole, although it's been speculated that it's purpose is largely ceremonial.\n \n
en.monolith_scientific_outfit_description: The Monolith scientific suit includes a flak vest of unknown origin and also features unique modifications for greater resistance to radiation at the cost of being more vulnerable to psy-fields. The anomaly protection system otherwise matches the SEVA bodysuit. Curiously, according to some legends, these suits are most often worn by the soldiers who defend the Brain Scorcher in Radar, although no one outside the Monolith faction can easily confirm.\n \n
en.mutant_boar_chop_description:
The most popular meat in the Zone. Nutritious and hardly contaminated. The scientists have enough samples already, which limits its use as a commodity. However, it can also serve as a source of animal fat for gun lubrication.
 
 WARNING:
 
Stalkers are advised to refrain from eating raw meat as animals and mutants in the Zone are likely to be carrying trichinosis or brucellosis. These diseases are transmitted by ingesting infected meat. The symptoms include fever, fatigue and appetite loss, all of which are extremely dangerous given the conditions in the Zone. Abscesses, fungal growth and tumours on the surface of the body indicate that the meat is infected and should be disposed of.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  low-tier mutant meat
  •  cookable
  •  toxic food
en.mutant_boar_leg_description:
Hoofs of boars can be used to produce various drugs, for example anti-radiation serums.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  common scientific specimen
en.mutant_brain_burer_description:
Burer have a perfect telepathic skills, which are absolutely unstudied by the science. That's why most of his organs are desirable by scientists and mercenaries. The brain, as the main source of his telepathic abilities, has a top-priority in this list. It's mass is a 0.5-1.5 times bigger than a human brain. There's no any external differences. Because of its size and structure the brain can be easily damaged during extraction: wasted organ can't be used in any scientific researches.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  rare scientific specimen
  •  valuable
en.mutant_burer_hand_description:
Burer's hand seems to play a vital role in the creature's use of the telekinetic powers. Scientists will gladly examine it in the hope of finding the source of mutation. Smells like old socks and bad kimchee.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  very rare scientific specimen
  •  valuable
en.mutant_cat_claw_description:
Cat's claws have an unknown structure, due to which they can easily cut jumpsuits. Scientists and military believe that a thorough study of them will help to create new highly durable materials and armour based on them.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  common scientific specimen
en.mutant_cat_tail_description:
Cats in the Zone are bigger than their pet cousins and are in fact very dangerous predators, capable of moving swiftly and without a sound, no matter the surface. Within the stalker community, a cat's tail is a symbol of awareness.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  common scientific specimen
en.mutant_cat_thyroid_description:
Long-term residence in radiation-contaminated territories resulted in the fact that the thyroid gland of local cats has multiplied in size. At the moment the hyperthyroidism after the change of generations is not acquired, but is a hereditary disease. Symptoms: increased food intake, increased activity, behaviour changes, nervousness, hair loss. These acquired skills are not destroyed, but rather allowed the mutated animals to quickly displace their healthy relatives and to occupy a high position in the food chain. Great interest for scientists is not due to the study of mechanisms and effects of disease.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  common scientific specimen
en.mutant_chimera_claw_description:
The claw of a chimera. Its exact molecular composition is unknown, but we know for a fact that it is as durable and sharp as a diamond, which explains why the beast is able to cut through armour with ease.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  very rare scientific specimen
  •  valuable
en.mutant_chimera_cogot_description:
The horn of a chimera. Its exact molecular composition is unknown, but we know for a fact that its tip is as durable and sharp as a diamond, which explains why the beast is able to cut through armour with ease. This item is in high demand as it is visibly similar to a rhino horn. Rumour has it that once upon a time a few poachers arrived in the Zone with the aim of acquiring the horn and selling it on the black market. To their surprise, however, chimeras did not make such easy targets as rhinos, so the poachers were never heard from again. All that remains of them are some unpleasant memories and belongings which indicate where they must have been ripped to pieces by these vicious mutants. Chimera’s horn can also be used as a crude sharpening tool, but utmost caution should be exercised because it can break, preventing the careless stalker from selling this valuable specimen.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  very rare scientific specimen
  •  valuable
en.mutant_chimera_meat_description:
A heavy chunk of chimera meat. Since chimeras are considered one of the most dangerous creatures, their meat is highly valued by veteran stalkers who are willing to buy it for fairly high prices. The cook on Skadovsk uses this meat as one of the ingredients of the so called masculine meal, although only few claim that it tastes better than boar meat. Can be sold to the scientists. Furthermore, it provides the best quality animal fat available in the Zone which can be used for the purposes of very effective gun lubrication.
 
 WARNING:
 
Stalkers are advised to refrain from eating raw meat as animals and mutants in the Zone are likely to be carrying trichinosis or brucellosis. These diseases are transmitted by ingesting infected meat. The symptoms include fever, fatigue and appetite loss, all of which are extremely dangerous given the conditions in the Zone. Abscesses, fungal growth and tumours on the surface of the body indicate that the meat is infected and should be disposed of.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  high-tier mutant meat
  •  cookable
  •  toxic food
en.mutant_controller_hand_description:
The controller seems to use its hand when mentally attacking its prey. The scientists will gladly examine it in the hope of finding the source of mutation responsible for its psy-related powers. Smells like old socks and bad kimchee.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  very rare scientific specimen
  •  valuable
en.mutant_dog_heart_description:
Blind dogs have many traits, much different from healthy counterparts. One of these differences - a very hardy heart, allowing the mutant for a long time to chase down its prey, until it's exhausted. Pulse when on the pursuit reaches 250 beats per minute, which is unthinkable for non-atherogenic healthy individuals of the same weight category. This endurance makes the blind dog's heart an interesting subject for study, but because of its prevalence, the rates for it among scientists are very small.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  common scientific specimen
en.mutant_dog_liver_description:
The liver of a dog, having undergone severe changes due to a toxic diet and a lack of clean drinking water in the Zone. The liver is of particular interest to scientists due to its potential for aiding research in medicine for liver diseases.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  the mutated part of the body
  •  common scientific instance
en.mutant_dog_meat_description:
Meat of mutated dogs is used as a cheap alternative to the more popular boar meat. The scientists have enough samples already, which limits its use as a commodity. However, it can also serve as a source of animal fat for gun lubrication.
 
 WARNING:
 
Stalkers are advised to refrain from eating raw meat as animals and mutants in the Zone are likely to be carrying trichinosis or brucellosis. These diseases are transmitted by ingesting infected meat. The symptoms include fever, fatigue and appetite loss, all of which are extremely dangerous given the conditions in the Zone. Abscesses, fungal growth and tumours on the surface of the body indicate that the meat is infected and should be disposed of.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  low-tier mutant meat
  •  cookable
  •  toxic food
en.mutant_dog_tail_description:
Resistance of mutant dogs' skin to electricity and chemical agents has been the object of scientific interest for a long time. Due to the absence of fat tissue, the tail is especially suited for lab tests.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  common scientific specimen
en.mutant_fang_psevdodog_description:
Pseudodogs fangs are used for tearing and holding their prey. Due to the tremendous jaw pressure of the mutant, it bites through most of the protective equipment used in the Zone. The teeth themselves are not too strong and are routinely replaced throughout life. Scientists are not particularly interested in them, unlike collectors from the outside of the Zone.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  common scientific specimen
en.mutant_flesh_eye_description:
Mutated, swollen eye of a flesh has a number of strange characteristics, the most interesting and potentially useful of which is the ability to repair even severe damage sustained to the organ. The specifics of this process are hitherto unknown, although the discovery of the mechanism behind it would most certainly revolutionize modern medicine by providing cures for many diseases. You can play a part in speeding up the progress of science and save the lives of innocent people by selling eyes of fleshes to the scientists.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  common scientific specimen
en.mutant_flesh_meat_description:
A popular choice in the Zone, flesh meat has a good taste and high nutritional value. The scientists have enough samples already, which limits its use as a commodity. However, it can also serve as a source of animal fat for gun lubrication.
 
 WARNING:
 
Stalkers are advised to refrain from eating raw meat as animals and mutants in the Zone are likely to be carrying trichinosis or brucellosis. These diseases are transmitted by ingesting infected meat. The symptoms include fever, fatigue and appetite loss, all of which are extremely dangerous given the conditions in the Zone. Abscesses, fungal growth and tumours on the surface of the body indicate that the meat is infected and should be disposed of.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  low-tier mutant meat
  •  cookable
  •  toxic food
en.mutant_fracture_hand_description:
A severed hand of a fracture - an extremely deformed humanoid mutant. It is certain that these creatures used to be humans and that they are the direct result of the second Chernobyl incident. From the remains of their clothing we can deduce that fractures originate from prisoner population that was stationed in the Exclusion Zone and sentenced to forced labour. Since all fractures suffer almost the same type of deformation of the body, it is supposed that when the exposure took place all subjects were in the same body position - either because they were being transported at the time or because someone forced them. All specimens encountered so far were male and sterile. Hence it can be safely assumed that the fracture population is unable to reproduce, and their number in the Zone will be steadily dwindling. For this reason, at present they remain one of the most rarely encountered types of mutant. The right hand of a fracture is a valuable research object.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  rare scientific specimen
  •  valuable
en.mutant_heart_bloodsucker_description:
Bloodsucker's heart have an unstudied ability - it secretes special enzymes which halt the coagulation process caused by incompatible blood types. Thanks to that, clotting does not occur even if the blood groups are different. The studies on the way this mechanism works and the composition of the enzyme will allow scientists to solve the problem of blood donor for patients with rare blood groups, as well as, probably, will open new doors for medicine.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  rare scientific specimen
  •  valuable
en.mutant_heart_chimera_description:
Fitting the creature's doubly nature, this heart of a chimera has grown into a doubled organ that can function as a reserve blood supply. Of interest to scientists is its possession of a high level of regeneration, providing the chimera with continued abilities in combat even after sustaining heavy injuries. It is being sought after to aid in the study of death rates among Siamese twins.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  rare scientific specimen
  •  valuable
en.mutant_krovosos_jaw_description:
Bloodsucker tentacles contain a special gland which produces an enzyme capable of dissolving the skin and simultaneously preventing blood from clotting. Discovering the enzyme's composition could be the cornerstone of a whole series of great medical advances. Some stalkers tend to use bloodsucker teeth to make necklaces and charms. It is thus safe to say that bloodsucker jaw is one of the most popular pieces of jewelry found in the Zone.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  rare scientific specimen
  •  valuable
en.mutant_krovosos_meat_description:
Bloodsucker meat is stringy and thus not suited for eating. Although bloodsuckers are fearsome beasts, the price of the meat is not very high due to its limited use. Can be sold to the scientists. Moreover, it can also serve as a source of animal fat for gun lubrication.
 
 WARNING:
 
Stalkers are advised to refrain from eating raw meat as animals and mutants in the Zone are likely to be carrying trichinosis or brucellosis. These diseases are transmitted by ingesting infected meat. The symptoms include fever, fatigue and appetite loss, all of which are extremely dangerous given the conditions in the Zone. Abscesses, fungal growth and tumours on the surface of the body indicate that the meat is infected and should be disposed of.
 
  •  high-tier mutant meat
  •  cookable
  •  toxic food
en.mutant_lurker_eye_description:
A mutated, yellow eye of a lurker. The glow surrounding the big pupil made the lurker's eye a matter of interest among local hunters. The scientists still look for potential uses of the lurker's eye.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  common scientific specimen
en.mutant_lurker_meat_description:
A good chunk of lurker meat. Nutritious and hardly contaminated. Can be sold to the scientists. However, it can also serve as a source of animal fat for gun lubrication.
 
 WARNING:
 
Stalkers are advised to refrain from eating raw meat as animals and mutants in the Zone are likely to be carrying trichinosis or brucellosis. These diseases are transmitted by ingesting infected meat. The symptoms include fever, fatigue and appetite loss, all of which are extremely dangerous given the conditions in the Zone. Abscesses, fungal growth and tumours on the surface of the body indicate that the meat is infected and should be disposed of.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  high-tier mutant meat
  •  cookable
  •  toxic food
en.mutant_lurker_tail_description:
Scientists suggest that the characteristics of a lurker tail is proof that lurkers are a mutant species of tushkano.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  common scientific specimen
en.mutant_part_controller_brain_tissue_desc:
Controllers have potent mental powers and it's safe to say that these powers have their origin in their brain. What's interesting, every mutant possessing abilities related to psy waves has a brain of similar structure. The scientists will pay handsomely for this tissue. It has a smell reminiscent of a sour pumpkin.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  very rare scientific specimen
  •  extremely valuable
en.mutant_pseudogigant_eye_description:
An eye of the pseudogiant has the appearance of a larger human eye, but with a few differences. It has a multi-coloured iris and its tear glands are exceptionally well-developed. Pseudogiants are known to lacrimate profusely and regularly, but we don't know whether this is a result of some physiological process or a by-product of the psychology of the creature. In any case, the scientists would be very eager to lay their hands on it, so they're willing to pay a high price for any specimen.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  very rare scientific specimen
  •  valuable
en.mutant_pseudogigant_hand_description:
A hand of a pseudogiant may prove useful for the scientists who will gladly examine it. Smells like old socks and bad kimchee.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  very rare scientific specimen
  •  valuable
en.mutant_psevdodog_meat_description:
Pseudodog meat is a common dietary component in the Zone. Tasty, with high nutritional value, a popular choice among stalkers. Can be sold to the scientists. Moreover, it can also serve as a source of animal fat for gun lubrication.
 
 WARNING:
 
Stalkers are advised to refrain from eating raw meat as animals and mutants in the Zone are likely to be carrying trichinosis or brucellosis. These diseases are transmitted by ingesting infected meat. The symptoms include fever, fatigue and appetite loss, all of which are extremely dangerous given the conditions in the Zone. Abscesses, fungal growth and tumours on the surface of the body indicate that the meat is infected and should be disposed of.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  low-tier mutant meat
  •  cookable
  •  toxic food
en.mutant_psevdodog_tail_description:
Rumour has it that a certain trend has recently taken over the fashion world: fur from the tails of pseudodogs. The fur is reportedly soft like no other. It is also said that it will keep you warm even in most vicious snowstorms and blizzards. For these reasons, pseudodog tails are in high demand.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  common scientific specimen
en.mutant_psysucker_hand_description:
Psysuckers are known for their skinny hands and long fingers. It seems that the skin around the fingertips is badly torn, which explains all the blood covering the fingers. The scientists will gladly examine it in the hope of finding the source of mutation responsible for its psy-related powers.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  common scientific specimen
en.mutant_psysucker_meat_description:
Similar to bloodsuckers, Psysucker meat is stringy and thus not suited for eating. The price of the meat is not very high due to its limited use. Can be sold to the scientists. Moreover, it can also serve as a source of animal fat for gun lubrication.
 
 WARNING:
 
Stalkers are advised to refrain from eating raw meat as animals and mutants in the Zone are likely to be carrying trichinosis or brucellosis. These diseases are transmitted by ingesting infected meat. The symptoms include fever, fatigue and appetite loss, all of which are extremely dangerous given the conditions in the Zone. Abscesses, fungal growth and tumours on the surface of the body indicate that the meat is infected and should be disposed of.
 
  •  high-tier mutant meat
  •  cookable
  •  toxic food
en.mutant_snork_hand_description:
As is widely known, snorks originate from humans, so eating their meat is considered cannibalistic and immoral. However, certain factions, such as bandits and mercenaries, include it in their meals so as to show their superiority over other groups. Can be sold to the scientists who make better use of it.
 
 WARNING:
 
Stalkers are advised to refrain from eating raw meat as animals and mutants in the Zone are likely to be carrying trichinosis or brucellosis. These diseases are transmitted by ingesting infected meat. The symptoms include fever, fatigue and appetite loss, all of which are extremely dangerous given the conditions in the Zone. Abscesses, fungal growth and tumours on the surface of the body indicate that the meat is infected and should be disposed of.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  low-tier mutant meat
  •  cookable
  •  toxic food
en.mutant_snork_leg_description:
Snork feet and legs contain a huge number of highly elastic tendons. This explains the beast's ability to perform its characteristic long leaps at its prey. The scientists still look for potential uses of the snork's leg, and will be grateful for each specimen you bring to their base.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  common scientific specimen
en.mutant_snork_mask_description:
Masks worn by snorks are apparently a version of GP-5, a Soviet gas mask, which was commonly used by soldiers as well as research and utility crews operating in the Zone when the second Chernobyl disaster occurred. Some of these people were working in numerous underground labouratories in which they were sealed from the outside world and exposed to tremendous amounts of radiation, which produced a creature we know as the snork. The mask, although broken beyond use (with cracked lenses and punctured filter hose), is still in demand by the scientific community. It can also be used to repair parts of outfits, but this process results in all remaining organic material being lost.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  technological item
  •  broken beyond repair
  •  common scientific specimen
en.mutant_tushkano_head_description:
The head of a tushkano. The scientists would like to study it in order to identify which creature tushkanos originate from.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  common scientific specimen
en.mutant_tushkano_meat_description:
A small portion of a mutated rodent's body. A cheap and commonly found food that is said to strengthen your heart after being cooked in open fire. Can be sold to the scientists.
 
 WARNING:
 
Stalkers are advised to refrain from eating raw meat as animals and mutants in the Zone are likely to be carrying trichinosis or brucellosis. These diseases are transmitted by ingesting infected meat. The symptoms include fever, fatigue and appetite loss, all of which are extremely dangerous given the conditions in the Zone. Abscesses, fungal growth and tumours on the surface of the body indicate that the meat is infected and should be disposed of.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  low-tier mutant meat
  •  cookable
  •  toxic food
en.mutant_tusk_boar_description:
Posing a considerable threat to anything caught in its strike, this fang spans a length of 30 centimetres. It is of no particular interest to scientists in the Zone due to its lackluster composition and strength. The fang is commonly used as a trophy among stalkers and has found its way as a collector's item outside of the Zone.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  common scientific specimen
en.mutant_zombi_hand_description:
This is a hand of a creature called old zombie - former human beings who lived or worked in the Exclusion Zone and were subjected to extreme amounts of radiation and psy-influence when the second Chernobyl incident occurred. After many years of being in this state, they possess only a frail body with bones and half-rotten flesh clearly visible. For this reason, the creatures very much resemble re-animated dead bodies, however they are very much alive and almost permanently hungry and thus can become a threat when encountered in packs. Severed hand not only constitutes a scientific research material but in many cases can also be used to identify the person to whom the hand belonged.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  mutated body part
  •  common scientific specimen
en.nbc_dolg_outfit_description: Artefact hunters among the Duty faction are rare, but this suit - based on standard NBC designs - exists to facilitate their lives and fulfill their obligations to aid the Ecologist faction. Compared to the standard PS5-M suit, this outfit boasts an integrated GP-5 gas mask as well as belt slots for AAMs and a highly efficient weight distribution system to help carry all the required equipment. Not the best suit in its class, it tends to be replaced once the Dutyer in question has the rank required for something better.\n \n
en.nbc_freedom_outfit_description: Based on the NBC suit designs used by neutral stalkers, this outfit is a variant made by Freedom's local craftsmen. While not on par with the standard NBC in some areas, the faction nevertheless managed to make an outstanding breakthrough in one area - chemical protection. This suit is something of a favourite among Freedomers who want to explore the Zone's myriad ruins without fear of chemical or radiological threats, especially when its highly efficient weight distribution system is factored in. Comes with an integrated GP-5 gas mask as standard.\n \n
en.nbc_merc_outfit_description: A CBRN suit with a kevlar vest, primarily used by Mercs on a budget. Outfits like these are mass-produced in factories outside the Zone for use in long-term military operations and disaster clean-ups all over the world. It comes standard with a GP-5 gas mask using a hose leading to a modern filter. Although clearly not made with the Zone's specific conditions in mind, it's nonetheless a popular choice among those who appreciate efficient weight distribution systems, although this model sports sub-par environmental protection compared to most locally made suits.\n \n
en.nbc_outfit_description: A budget CBRN suit with a kevlar vest. Outfits like these are mass-produced in factories outside the Zone for use in long-term military operations and disaster clean-ups all over the world. It comes standard with a GP-5 gas mask using a hose leading to a modern filter. Although clearly not made with the Zone's specific conditions in mind, it's nonetheless a popular choice among those who appreciate efficient weight distribution systems, and the overall protection is still comparable to an average stalker suit.\n \n
en.nomad_outfit_description: A Zone-assembled suit that has been handmade by local craftsmen as a matter of survival. This particular outfit is of high quality and feels exceptionally light and comfortable to wear, despite being much more protective overall when compared to other stalker suits. It is clear that whoever assembled this armour was highly familiar with the Zone's nature, and evidently put a great deal of pride and care into their work - something that might not have been true of its previous owner. Either way, its quality is matched only by its price.\n \n
en.nosorog_outfit_description: A next generation exoskeleton, consisting of a doubly reinforced exosuit and an armoured Czech-M10 gas mask. This suit is rumoured to have been the brainchild of a now deceased stalker named "Fang", whose designs were later claimed by all sorts of powerful factions across the Zone. If regular exoskeletons allow stalkers to feel like a light vehicle, the Nosorog almost makes them believe they're a main battle tank, able to withstand incredible punishment while carrying tremendous ordnance. Typically only available to a faction's most valuable members, and even then only during exceptionally dangerous missions.\n \n
en.novice_outfit_camo_description: A warm leather anorak worn over a cosy turtleneck sweater, tucked into sturdy jeans and hiking boots. Formerly owned by a typical firearm enthusiast, it sports several advantages over an ordinary jacket - namely a camouflage pattern and a higher quality material choice alongside a more educated weight distribution system. Still utterly inadequate for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n
en.novice_outfit_description: A warm leather anorak worn over a cosy turtleneck sweater, tucked into sturdy jeans and hiking boots. The outfit of choice among naive or inexperienced stalkers, veterans consider it barely a step above walking around the Zone in your underwear.\n \n
en.novice_outfit_tourist_description: A warm leather anorak with an integrated backpack, worn over a cosy turtleneck sweater, tucked into sturdy jeans and hiking boots. It seems the previous owner was a real lover for hikes. The educated choice of materials and weight management system makes this more useful than most leather jackets, but is still unsuitable for anything more than quiet walks along the Zone's borders.\n \n
en.pri_decoder_docs_conclusion_txt: The manuscript contains a short report on how Fang and Strelok infiltrated a hidden laboratory and their journey there. It makes mention of large Monolith presence in and around the Chernobyl NPP. More specifically under the concrete sarcophagus, also suggesting it may be their base of operations but with the intent to hide something. Reading further it states that they discovered laboratory hidden further in the north under the generators, however they were unable to gain access at the time of writing as they required the use of a decoder.
en.quest_package_desc:
The content of this package is unknown.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  personal belongings
  •  valuable
en.recover_mutant_data_device_desc: A sturdy device that was planted on a mutant to monitor its movements, habits and vital signs. Shows signs of corrosion, and is covered in numerous bite and scratch marks.
en.redline_novice_outfit_description: A snazzy bright red leather anorak worn over a cosy turtleneck sweater, tucked into sturdy jeans and hiking boots. The colour alone should be reason enough to avoid use in the Zone.\n \n
en.renegade_exo_outfit_description: One of a few exoskeletons assembled by the Renegades back in 2011 to help secure their final push into Clear Sky's base. Unfortunately, they were virtually wiped out before their final victory ever came, thanks to the interference of a Mercenary with a trench coat and a marksman rifle. Modern Renegades who wear this armour always go into battle like a man possessed, eager to mete vengeance upon their most hated foes. That is to say, almost everyone else.\n \n
en.renegade_scientific_outfit_description: This comfortable bodysuit, originally intended for research in the Zone, makes for a great alternative to the various Light Combat or hand-made suits one might have access to. Provides genuinely excellent protection against thermal and chemical hazards, along with good protection against blunt trauma. Still, for a SEVA suit, this armour is fairly mediocre overall.\n \n
en.renegademerc_outfit_description: A light combat suit obtained through shady means to help armour local groups of Renegades. Its design is based on the suit used by the special forces of Western armies. Surprisingly pleasant to wear, this outfit provides more than adequate protection from small arms, lacerations and errant shrapnel. Still, it is not really intended for use in areas of high radiological or anomalous activity, and as such few wearers will be found deep in the Zone with any regularity.\n \n
en.scientific_outfit_description: This suit was produced by one of Kiev's defence research institutes and represents an excellent alternative to hand-made stalker suits. It combines a closed cycle breathing system and an integrated system of anomalous field suppression with a more than adequate ballistic vest. Due to the perfect materials, this suit is a good choice. Still, it is expensive, and those who prefer pitched battle over careful anomaly investigation are advised to look elsewhere.\n \n
en.special_delivery_case_desc: Sealed case that contains undisclosed contents. Rather heavy.
en.specops_bandit_outfit_description: A set of PS5 series body armour with beryllium sputter, acquired through shady means by the Zone's Bandits. Although the suit shows visible signs of irreparable wear and tear, it's still an excellent choice for battle when both protection and mobility is needed. Not really recommended for use near anomalies, however.\n \n
en.specops_dolg_outfit_description: This item comprises military PS5 series body armour with beryllium sputter modified for use in the Zone. Compared to a standard PS5-M universal protection suit, this outfit is more ergonomic and protective overall, but is slightly less insulated and provides much less defence against heavy impacts, like a poltergeist's telekinetic attack.\n \n
en.specops_merc_outfit_description: Spetsnaz body armour "acquired" and repurposed by the Zone's Mercenaries. Provides good physical protection while also being remarkably comfortable to wear, although its weaknesses against environmental hazards demand it be used alongside a high quality gas mask and helmet. Often worn by Mercenaries who used to work for the SSU.\n \n
en.specops_outfit_description: This item comprises military PS5 series body armour with beryllium sputter modified for use in the Zone. Designed for intense rapid-insertion assault operations, this outfit is standard issue for most Spetsnaz operators in the Zone, but requires a high quality respirator to counteract its weaknesses against radiation and anomalies. Occasionally, this suit will be sold to ordinary stalkers in exchange for various "favours".\n \n
en.st_addon_scope_0kp2_descr: The original design of OKP sights allows firing from unstable positions in rapidly changing conditions, reduces the operator fatigue, and, if necessary, allows unhindered use of base mechanical sights. The key feature of this sight is a rimless reflector attachment that doesn't block any field of fire sections; instead, reflector is protected by thin and durable visor that is almost un-noticeable when firing. This variant is installed on the dovetail type mount.
en.st_addon_scope_1p29_descr:
PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Russia
  •  Manufacturer: Soviet Union
  •  magnification: 4.0x
en.st_addon_scope_1p59_descr:
PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Russia
  •  Manufacturer: Various Manufacturers
  •  magnification: 3.0x - 10.0x
 
  •  LED Toggle
en.st_addon_scope_1p76_descr: The 1P76 optical sight designed for battlefield surveillance, target selection, target ranging and aimed fire against luminous and illuminated targets during the day, at dusk and at night. The sights have a wide field of view that allows for quick terrain observation in a fast-moving combat situation simultaneously with the naked eye and through the sight without loss of target visibility.
en.st_addon_scope_1p78gs_descr:
PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Russia
  •  Manufacturer: NPZ Factory
  •  magnification: 2.8x
en.st_addon_scope_1p87_descr:
PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Russia
  •  Manufacturer: Valday
  •  Magnification: 1.0x
en.st_addon_scope_1pn93_descr:
PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Russia
  •  Manufacturer: NPZ Optics
  •  magnification: 4.0x
 
  •  Night Vision Toggle
en.st_addon_scope_1pn93n2_1gs_descr:
PROPERTIES:
  •  Origin: Russia
  •  Manufacturer: Russia
  •  magnification: 4.0x
 
  •  Night Vision Toggle
en.st_addon_scope_226sig_kit: Tactical Kit for Sig Sauer P226R
en.st_addon_scope_226sig_kit_descr:
COMPATIBILITY:
 
 •  Sig Sauer P226R pistol
 
 The kit includes: Threaded barrel for using with suppressor, ergonomic gripp plates and Trijicon RMR Red Dot sight with mounting plate. This upgrade is irreversible.
en.st_addon_scope_23_up_descr:
COMPATIBILITY:
 
 •  KS-23 Carabine
 
 Short and light barrel for KS-23 Carabine. This upgrade is irreversible.
en.st_addon_scope_5c_tik_descr:

en.st_addon_scope_compm4s_descr: The Aimpoint® CompM4s sight, with low battery compartment, belongs to the CompM4 series of sights which are the finest sights Aimpoint has ever produced.
