  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor (after modification)
en.st_wpn_l96a1_descr:
The Accuracy International Arctic Warfare rifle is a family of bolt-action sniper rifles designed and manufactured by the British company, Accuracy International. It has proved popular as a civilian, police and military rifle since its introduction in the 1980s. The rifles have some features which improve performance in very cold conditions, without impairing operation in less extreme conditions.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Leupold Mark 4 scope (integrated)
en.st_wpn_l96a1m_descr:
The Accuracy International Arctic Warfare rifle is a family of bolt-action sniper rifles designed and manufactured by the British company, Accuracy International. It has proved popular as a civilian, police and military rifle since its introduction in the 1980s. The rifles have some features which improve performance in very cold conditions, without impairing operation in less extreme conditions. L96 AWSM utilises .338 Lapua Magnum.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Leupold Mark 4 scope (integrated)
en.st_wpn_lr300_descr:
Based on the AR-15, the LR-300 is an excellent weapon system suitable for military use in close quarters battle. Its folding skeletal stock makes it ideal for aggressive and rapid building entries requiring a compact yet powerful selective fire weapon. With excellent ergonomics and lightweight, this weapon is highly accurate but it is sensitive to dirt and is not ideal for difficult field conditions.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, EOTech scopes
  •  M203 grenade launcher (after modification)
en.st_wpn_m16_descr:
The M16 is the U.S. military designation for a family of rifles derived from the ArmaLite AR-15 and further developed by Colt starting in the 1960s. The M16 rifle family including the M16/A1/A2/A3/A4 has been the primary infantry rifle of the United States military since the 1960s. With its variants, it has been in use by 15 NATO countries, and is the most produced firearm in its calibre. The M16 is a lightweight, 5.56x45mm calibre, air cooled, gas operated, magazine fed assault rifle, with a rotating bolt, actuated by direct impingement gas operation. The rifle is made of steel, aluminium, and composite plastics.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, EOTech scopes
  •  M203 grenade launcher
en.st_wpn_m16a2_descr:
The M16A2 rifle entered service in the 1980s, chambered to fire the standard NATO round, the Belgian designed M855/M856 cartridge. The M16A2 is a select fire rifle, incorporating design elements requested by the Marine Corps: an adjustable, windage rear sight, longer stock, heavier barrel, case deflector for left-hand shooters and cylindrical hand guards. The M16A2 is manufactured by Colt and the Fabrique Nationale de Herstal arms company, with the variant rifles made elsewhere in the world.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  M203 grenade launcher
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
en.st_wpn_m249_descr:
The M249 is a belt fed 5.56x45 machine gun. It is the American adaptation of the Belgian FN Minimi. The M249 has seen action in every major conflict involving the United States since the U.S. invasion of Panama in 1989. Soldiers are generally satisfied with the weapon's performance, though there have been reports of clogging with dirt and sand.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, EOTech scopes
en.st_wpn_m24_descr:
A sniper rifle of the US Army, the M24 Sniper Weapon System (SWS) is based on the Remington 700. Fitted with a Leupold 10x40mm scope, the M24 has a stainless steel barrel and an adjustable composite stock made from Kevlar and graphite.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  M24 sniper scope (integrated)
en.st_wpn_m4_butcher_descr:
Modernized model Colt M4A1 Commando (CQBR) is an assault rifle, designed in the USA on the basis of AR-15 platform for 5.56x45 NATO cartridge. It was developed primarily for use by vehicle operators and fireteams, who required a more compact and lighter weapon than a standard full-length rifle, but using the same ordnance. However, US SOCOM (United States Special Operations Command) was soon to adopt the M4A1 for all subordinate units, followed by general introduction of M4A1 into service in the US Army and Marine Corps.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All for picatinny/weaver (except of Red Dot Sights with low mount: Trijicon RMR, Leupold DeltaPoint PRO, Aimpoint T1 (low mount), Docter Sight, 1P76 Rakurs (low mount) and OKP-7.
  •  Silencer: NATO
en.st_wpn_m4_descr:
The M4 Carbine descends from a family of firearms tracing its lineage back to earlier carbine versions of the M16, all based on the original AR-15 made by ArmaLite. It is a shorter and lighter version of the M16 assault rifle, sharing 80% of its parts. The M4 fires 5.56x45mm NATO ammunition and are gas operated, air cooled, magazine fed, selective fire firearms with a 4 position telescoping stock. As with many carbines, the M4 is handy and more convenient to carry than a full length rifle. It has been adopted by United States Special Operations Command (USSOCOM) and is the preferred weapon of the U.S. Army Special Forces and Navy SEALs.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, EOTech scopes
  •  M203 grenade launcher
en.st_wpn_m4_ru556_descr:
Several of these rifles entered the Zone through scientists. According to legend, Professor Sakharov received 6 small conteiners with these guns from foreign sponsors, but 5 of them were lost during transportation. Ruger SR-556 is an American self-loading carbine manufactured by the arms company Sturm, Ruger & Company, Inc. became the first Ruger of the AR-10 family. Ruger SR-762 chambered for .308. Belongs to a few samples of AR-15 rifles using the removal of powder gases. Production ceased in January 2016 after the introduction of the Keymod Lightweight option.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Universal mount by NcStar with Trijicon RMR red dot sight installed on it. 
  •  Scopes: All for picatinny/weaver (except of Red Dot Sights with low mount: Trijicon RMR, Leupold DeltaPoint PRO, Aimpoint T1 (low mount), Docter Sight, 1P76 Rakurs (low mount).\n  •  Suppressor: NATO
en.st_wpn_m4_skryaga_descr:
Like Beard, a merchant from Zaton, in his time, Skryaga also decided to earn extra money by selling carbines in "his" body kit. The result was a good, light and very ergonomic barrel based on the M4.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All for picatinny/weaver (except of Red Dot Sights with low mount: Trijicon RMR, Leupold DeltaPoint PRO, Aimpoint T1 (low mount), Docter Sight, 1P76 Rakurs (low mount) and MarchF).
  •  Suppressor: KAC QDSS NT-4 5.56х45
  •  M203 grenade launcher
en.st_wpn_m4_tac_descr:
Colt M4A1 - an assault rifle (carbine), designed in the USA on the basis of AR-15 platform for 5.56x45 NATO cartridge. It was developed primarily for use by vehicle operators and fireteams, who required a more compact and lighter weapon than a standard full-length rifle, but using the same ordnance. However, US SOCOM (United States Special Operations Command) was soon to adopt the M4A1 for all subordinate units, followed by general introduction of M4A1 into service in the US Army and Marine Corps.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All for picatinny/weaver (except of Red Dot Sights with low mount: Trijicon RMR, Leupold DeltaPoint PRO, Aimpoint T1 (low mount), Docter Sight, 1P76 Rakurs (low mount).
  •  Silencer: Surefire SOCOM556-MONSTER
en.st_wpn_m4a1_descr:
Ideal for CQC (Close Quarters Combat), the M4A1 carbine is a fully automatic variant of the M16A2 assault rifle. In military use, it has largely replaced the submachine guns firing pistol cartridges that are inadequate against modern body armour. The M4A1 is a modified variant of the M4 carbine able to fire automatic or semi-automatic.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, EOTech scopes
  •  M203 grenade launcher (after modification)
en.st_wpn_m4a1_siber_descr:
The M4 Carbine descends from a family of firearms tracing its lineage back to earlier carbine versions of the M16, all based on the original AR-15 made by ArmaLite. It is a shorter and lighter version of the M16 assault rifle, sharing 80% of its parts. The M4 fires 5.56x45mm NATO ammunition and are gas operated, air cooled, magazine fed, selective fire firearms with a 4 position telescoping stock. As with many carbines, the M4 is handy and more convenient to carry than a full length rifle. It has been adopted by United States Special Operations Command (USSOCOM) and is the preferred weapon of the U.S. Army Special Forces and Navy SEALs.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All for picatinny/weaver (except of Red Dot Sights with low mount: Trijicon RMR, Leupold DeltaPoint PRO, Aimpoint T1 (low mount), Docter Sight, 1P76 Rakurs (low mount) and OKP-7.
  •  Suppressor: ADVANCED ARMAMENT (AAC) M4-2000 
  •  M203 grenade launcher
en.st_wpn_m60_descr: The M60, officially the United States Machine Gun, Caliber 7.62 mm, M60, is a family of American general-purpose machine guns firing 7.62x51mm NATO cartridges from a disintegrating belt of M13 links. There are several types of ammunition approved for use in the M60, including ball, tracer, and armor-piercing rounds.\n \n
en.st_wpn_m79_descr: The M79 grenade launcher is a single shot, shoulder-fired, break-action grenade launcher that fires a 40x46mm grenade, which uses what the US Army calls the High-Low Propulsion System to keep recoil low. First appeared during the Vietnam War.
en.st_wpn_m82_descr: Barrett M82, in the documentation of the US Armed Forces M107 - American self-loading large-caliber sniper rifle, manufactured by Barrett Firearms Manufacturing and in service with several countries around the world. The automatics of this rifle are based on a butterfly valve. The rifle belongs to the class of "antimaterial", but is more often used as a weapon against enemy infantry.\n \n
en.st_wpn_m98b_descr:
The Barrett M98B is a bolt-action sniper rifle chambered in .338 Lapua Mag. Rather than modifying a hunting rifle, Barrett created an original design for the M98B. The M98B features an adjustable stock and cheek pad for comfort, an adjustable trigger for preferable trigger control, a rear monopod and a front bi-pod for stability. Users can further modify the rifle by attaching sights, lights and grips to its rails. The M98B has a simple design making it easy for users to disassemble and clean.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Leupold Mark 4 scope (integrated)
  •  SR-25 suppressor(after modification)
en.st_wpn_mk14_descr:
United States Navy Mark 14 Enhanced Battle Rifle (Mk 14 Enhanced Battle Rifle, "improved combat rifle") - American Marksman rifle, created by special operations of the US Navy based on the M14 rifle. Used by a number of special units of the Navy, including SEAL.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, C-more Railway Tactical, EOTech scopes
en.st_wpn_mosin_descr:
A basic version of the Mosin-Nagant with slightly worn interiors. The M1891/30 was produced for standard issue to all Soviet infantry from 1930 to 1945. It was extremely successful in World War II as a sniper rifle when it was equipped with a 4x PE or a 3.5x PU scope.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
•  PU scope
en.st_wpn_mossberg590_descr: Mossberg 590 was developed by O.F.Mossberg & sons company during 1970s in US as a modification of the Mossberg model 500 shotgun for adoption by the US Military. Modifications included strengthening of the receiver, installation of the vented heatshield around the barrel, a bayonet lug and extended magazine and an aluminium trigger guard instead of plastic one. Standard finish is Parkerizing, and the gun is provided with replacement pistol grip, which allows easy conversion to 'Cruiser' modification. Since 1987, this was the standard shotgun of US Armed forces; officially replaced in US Army with Benelli M1014 semi-automatic shotgun, Mossberg 590 shotguns are still used by US Navy and US Marine Corp.\n \n
en.st_wpn_mp133_bas_descr: The MP-133 is a product of the Izhevsk Mechanical Plant. This shotgun is an evolution of the earlier IZh-81 pump action shotgun, and is intended for civilian and security markets. It is produced in two basic versions: standard MP-133 with tubular magazine and MP-133K with detachable box magazine, both versions can be in various barrel lengths and styles, wood or polymer furniture, fixed or side folding polymer stock or pistol grip only. The MP-133 shotgun uses traditional pump action with sliding forend that has dual action bars. Locking is achieved by a tilting locking piece, installed in the bolt and engages the notch in the barrel extension when in battery. Feed is from tubular underbarrel magazine that holds 4 rounds, though capacity can be enhanced by installing magazine extension tubes.\n
en.st_wpn_mp133_descr: The MP-133 is a product of the Izhevsk Mechanical Plant. This shotgun is an evolution of the earlier IZh-81 pump action shotgun, and is intended for civilian and security markets. It is produced in two basic versions: standard MP-133 with tubular magazine and MP-133K with detachable box magazine, both versions can be in various barrel lengths and styles, wood or polymer furniture, fixed or side folding polymer stock or pistol grip only. The MP-133 shotgun uses traditional pump action with sliding forend that has dual action bars. Locking is achieved by a tilting locking piece, installed in the bolt and engages the notch in the barrel extension when in battery. Feed is from tubular underbarrel magazine that holds 4 rounds, though capacity can be enhanced by installing magazine extension tubes.\n \n
en.st_wpn_mp153_bas_descr: The MP-153 self-loading shotgun was the first semi-automatic shotgun designed and manufactured by Izhevsk Mechanical Plant. It was developed along with the pump action MP-133 shotgun and shares many parts and assemblies with it. The MP-153 is one of the most popular semi-automatic shotguns of Russian origin, and is widely used to hunt various games in Russia. It is also sold for export, it is known as Remington "Spartan" SPR-453 autoloader in US. MP-153 shotguns are known for solid but somewhat heavy construction and reliability.\n
en.st_wpn_mp153_descr: The MP-153 self-loading shotgun was the first semi-automatic shotgun designed and manufactured by Izhevsk Mechanical Plant. It was developed along with the pump action MP-133 shotgun and shares many parts and assemblies with it. The MP-153 is one of the most popular semi-automatic shotguns of Russian origin, and is widely used to hunt various games in Russia. It is also sold for export, it is known as Remington "Spartan" SPR-453 autoloader in US. MP-153 shotguns are known for solid but somewhat heavy construction and reliability.\n \n
en.st_wpn_mp153_m1_descr:
A customized version of the MP-153 shotgun. Capable of semi-automatic fire like its predecessor, but provides better recoil compensation thanks to the quite effective muzzle brake. It also has Picatinny rail which allows to use reflex and holographic sights and ability to use sound supressor.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: Due to the position of the stock, it is possible to install sights only with a very low mount: Barska AC10632, C-More, Docter Sight, Aimpoint T1, Trijicon RMR, Leupold DeltaPoint PRO, OKP-7, 1P76 Rakurs. 
 
  •  Silencer: SilencerCo Salvo® 12
en.st_wpn_mp412_descr: The MP412 REX is a Russian double-action .357 magnum revolver with a break-action chamber and an automatic ejector. It was designed for export (REX, Revolver for EXport), but never put into full production due to lack of a market. The weapon was designed by IZHMECH, the state-owned Russian Izhevsk Mechanical Plant, which also designed and produced the Makarov PM semi-automatic pistol.
en.st_wpn_mp5_descr:
The MP5 is one of the most famous and wide spread firearms of its class. The MP5's compactness, accuracy and reliability have made it a favourite of numerous military, law enforcement, intelligence and security organisations the world over. The MP5A3 is fitted with a retractable metal stock. Not surprisingly, having flooded the global black markets, it eventually made its way to the Zone.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632 scopes
  •  9x19 compatible suppressor (after modification)
en.st_wpn_mp5_nimble_descr:
A submachine gun prototype based on the excellent H&K MP5A3 model. Designed as a next generation close quarter combat weapon, it benefits from a higher calibre, rate of fire and accuracy.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632 scopes
  •  GemTech suppressor (after modification)
en.st_wpn_mp5sd_descr:
A variant of the MP5 with an integrated suppressor. In the Zone, mercenaries prefer this variant to the normal one.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632 scopes, EOTech scopes
  •  special MP5SD suppressor (integrated)
en.st_wpn_mp7_descr:
The Heckler & Koch MP7 (Maschinenpistole 7) is a German personal defence weapon (PDW) designed and manufactured by Heckler & Koch (H&K) and chambered for the HK 4.6x30mm cartridge. It was designed with the new cartridge to meet NATO requirements published in 1989, as these requirements call for a personal defense weapon (PDW) class firearm, with a greater ability to penetrate body armor than current weapons limited to conventional pistol cartridges. The MP7 went into production in 2001.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  9x19 compatible suppressor
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, C-more Railway Tactical, EOTech scopes
en.st_wpn_oc33_descr:
OTs-33 "Pernach" (Russian for multivaned mace) is a recent entry into the small class of Russian automatic pistols. The OTs-33 is a blowback-operated weapon firing the 9x18mm Makarov pistol cartridge in automatic and semi-automatic mode. The Pernach was developed in 1995 to replace the Stechkin APS pistol in special paramilitary units. Designed in 1995 by Igor Stechkin at the TSKIB SOO, Central Design Bureau for Sporting and Hunting arms, Tula, Russia, the same designer who developed the APS.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  APS suppressor (after modification)
en.st_wpn_p90_descr:
The FN P90 submachine gun was developed in the late 1980s as a personal defence weapon for troops whose primary activities does not include small arms, such as vehicle and tank crew members, artillery crews etc. Standard pistols and submachine guns were proven ineffective against enemies wearing body armour. FN designers developed a new round, initially designated as SS90, to achieve high penetration while keeping recoil low FN used a small-bore approach, creating a round that looked like the scaled down 5.56 NATO round. The result was FN P90 personal defence weapon, introduced in 1990 along with improved 5.7x28 SS190 ammunition which replaced polymer cored bullets with heavier dual core, steel and aluminium, bullets for better penetration against body armour.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, EOTech scopes
  •  5.7x28 compatible suppressor
en.st_wpn_pb_bas_descr:
The PB (Pistolet Besshumnyj or Silent Pistol), also known in the Soviet army as 6P9, has been developed in 1967 for Spetsnaz units of the Soviet Army, as well as for KGB operatives. The pistol is based on a modified Makarov PM pistol and features an unique integral two part suppressor. Issued into service in 1967, the PB (GRAU index 6P9) is a Soviet silenced pistol based on the Makarov PM. It was intended for use by the KGB and army reconnaissance groups. The front part of the suppressor is detachable, so that the pistol can be broken down into two parts for convenient carry. The PB's integral suppressor also reduces the bullet velocity to below subsonic levels.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  6P9 suppressor (integrated)
en.st_wpn_pb_descr:
The PB (Pistolet Besshumnyj or Silent Pistol), also known in the Soviet army as 6P9, has been developed in 1967 for Spetsnaz units of the Soviet Army, as well as for KGB operatives. The pistol is based on a modified Makarov PM pistol and features an unique integral two part suppressor. Issued into service in 1967, the PB (GRAU index 6P9) is a Soviet silenced pistol based on the Makarov PM. It was intended for use by the KGB and army reconnaissance groups. The front part of the suppressor is detachable, so that the pistol can be broken down into two parts for convenient carry. The PB's integral suppressor also reduces the bullet velocity to below subsonic levels.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  6P9 suppressor (integrated)
en.st_wpn_pk_siber_descr:
The PK (Pulemyot Kalashnikova, or "Kalashnikov's machine gun"), is a belt-fed general-purpose machine gun, chambered for the 7.62?54mmR rimmed cartridge. Designed in the Soviet Union and currently in production in Russia, the original PK machine gun was introduced in 1961 and the improved PKM variant was introduced in 1969. The PK was designed to replace the SGM and RP-46 machine guns that were previously in Soviet service. The weapon remains in use as a front-line infantry and vehicle-mounted weapon with Russia's armed forces and has also been exported extensively and produced in several other countries under license.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  None
en.st_wpn_pkm_siber_descr:
Modification of an army machine gun with belt feed. Despite the high mass and unsatisfactory accuracy, it has found a fairly wide application in the Zone. It is mainly used to suppress an enemy outnumbered and when clearing mutant lairs.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  None
en.st_wpn_pkm_zenit_shorty_descr:
A lighter and improved modification of the PKM machine gun in a body kit from the Zenitka company. Thanks to the short barrel, the model has improved ergonomics, which makes it more suitable for fighting in urban areas. The model has angled foregrip installed for better recoil control. In the Zone it is used mainly by the military (special forces).
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for picatinny/weaver, Zenit Perst-3 tactical device with red laser (included) 
  •  Silencer: TGP-V Suppressor
en.st_wpn_pkm_zenit_siber_descr:
An improved modification of the PKM machine gun in a body kit from the Zenitka company. The model has angled foregrip installed for better recoil control. In the Zone it is used mainly by the military (special forces).
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for picatinny/weaver, Zenit Perst-3 tactical device with red laser (included) 
  •  Silencer: TGP-V Suppressor
en.st_wpn_pkm_zulus_descr:
This weapon was previously owned by Zulu. Its special features include an enlarged ammo box and a particularly reliable retracting mechanism design. The modifications meant an increase in the weapon's weight, but that wasn't a problem for the last owner.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-1S-03 Kobra sight (integrated)
en.st_wpn_pkp_descr: The PKP "Pecheneg" is a Russian light machine gun chambered for the 7.62x54mmR round. It is a modernised PKM machine gun. The Pecheneg is said to be more accurate than all its predecessors due to a heavier, non-removable, forced air cooling barrel with radial cooling ribs and a handle which eliminates the haze effect from hot gases and keeps the barrel cooler, making the weapon more reliable.\n \n
en.st_wpn_pkp_siber_descr:
The PKP "Pecheneg" is a Russian light machine gun chambered for the 7.62x54mmR round. It is a modernised PKM machine gun. The Pecheneg is said to be more accurate than all its predecessors due to a heavier, non-removable, forced air cooling barrel with radial cooling ribs and a handle which eliminates the haze effect from hot gases and keeps the barrel cooler, making the weapon more reliable.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: all scopes for Dovetail mount
  •  Silencer: TGP-V Suppressor
en.st_wpn_pkp_tac_siber_descr:
A lighter and improved modification of the PKP machine gun. Thanks to the short barrel, the model has improved ergonomics, which makes it more suitable for fighting in urban areas. The model has angled foregrip installed for better recoil control. In the Zone it is used mainly by the military (special forces) and Mercenaries.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for picatinny/weaver, Zenit Perst-3 tactical device with red laser (included)
  •  Silencer: TGP-V Suppressor
en.st_wpn_pl15_descr:
Russian self-loading pistol PL-15-01 chambered for 9x19 mm Parabellum. The difference between the pistol and its analogues is its ergonomics and balance, which correspond to the current ideas about human biomechanics. The automation calculation methods used during its creation contribute to a large shooting resource, and in combination with the applied ergonomics solutions, make the pistol comfortable in terms of recoil, toss after a shot and quick return to the aiming line. All this contributes to a quick re-shot and fast transfer of fire, and an ergonomic handle with a large angle of inclination makes it easier to shoot "offhand".
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  The weapon can be upgraded using kit: "Tactic".
  •  The LAM for PL-15 can be installed
  •  Silencer: PL-15 Sound Suppressor
en.st_wpn_pl15_pl15_scolaz_descr:
Russian self-loading pistol PL-15-01 chambered for 9x19 mm Parabellum. The difference between the pistol and its analogues is its ergonomics and balance, which correspond to the current ideas about human biomechanics. The automation calculation methods used during its creation contribute to a large shooting resource, and in combination with the applied ergonomics solutions, make the pistol comfortable in terms of recoil, toss after a shot and quick return to the aiming line. All this contributes to a quick re-shot and fast transfer of fire, and an ergonomic handle with a large angle of inclination makes it easier to shoot "offhand".
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  The weapon was upgraded using: "Tactic" kit (LAM, Aimpoint T1 scope).
  •  Silencer: PL-15 Sound Suppressor
en.st_wpn_pm_actor_descr: An ancient PM given to Degtyarev as a reward for "Successful execution of highly dangerous state orders, as well as valour and courage shown in the line of duty".
en.st_wpn_pm_bas_actor_descr:
A semi-mythical PM with extended barrel and suppressor mount threading. Some claim it was a limited series manufactured in Bulgaria, others insist on DDR, and some are even prone to think of it as a handcrafted upgrade. How did it end up in the Zone, no living soul can tell...
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Silencer: Silencer 9mm
en.st_wpn_pm_bas_custom_descr:
Customized version of Makarov PM with longer threaded barrel for use with PMM silencer. This Soviet legacy is the most common pistol in the Zone and is one of the best compact self-defence handguns available. It is small, highly reliable, easy to use and maintain in working order. It also suffers from several drawbacks, including short range, low firing accuracy and poor stopping power, while also losing out to modern pistols in its class in terms of weight and magazine size. In 1951, the Pistol Makarova was selected because of its simplicity, easy manufacturing, accuracy, and reasonable stopping power.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Silencer: Silencer PMM
en.st_wpn_pm_bas_descr: This Soviet legacy is the most common pistol in the Zone and is one of the best compact self-defence handguns available. It is small, highly reliable, easy to use and maintain in working order. It also suffers from several drawbacks, including short range, low firing accuracy and poor stopping power, while also losing out to modern pistols in its class in terms of weight and magazine size. In 1951, the Pistol Makarova was selected because of its simplicity, easy manufacturing, accuracy, and reasonable stopping power.
en.st_wpn_pm_descr: This Soviet legacy is the most common pistol in the Zone and is one of the best compact self-defence handguns available. It is small, highly reliable, easy to use and maintain in working order. It also suffers from several drawbacks, including short range, low firing accuracy and poor stopping power, while also losing out to modern pistols in its class in terms of weight and magazine size. In 1951, the Pistol Makarova was selected because of its simplicity, easy manufacturing, accuracy, and reasonable stopping power.
en.st_wpn_pmm_bas_descr:
The most widely known variant, the Makarov PMM was a redesign of the original gun. In 1990 a group of engineers reworked the original Makarov, primarily by increasing the load for the cartridge. The result is 25% more gas pressure increasing in muzzle velocity. The PMM magazine holds 12 rounds, compared to the PM's 8 rounds.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Silencer: Silencer PMM
en.st_wpn_pmm_descr: The most widely known variant, the Makarov PMM was a redesign of the original gun. In 1990 a group of engineers reworked the original Makarov, primarily by increasing the load for the cartridge. The result is 25% more gas pressure increasing in muzzle velocity. The PMM magazine holds 12 rounds, compared to the PM's 8 rounds.
en.st_wpn_pp2000_descr:
The PP-2000 is the latest development of the Instrument Design Bureau (KBP) from Tula, Russia. First displayed to the public in 2004, the patent for its layout has been filed in 2001 and was issued in 2003. The niche for the PP-2000 is a personal defence weapon for non-infantry military personnel, as well as close combat weapon for special forces and special law enforcement teams. As such, it is a compact weapon with few parts for reliability, simplified maintenance and low cost. Best suited for close combat.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, EOTech scopes
  •  9x19 compatible suppressor
en.st_wpn_ppsh41_descr: A major infantry weapon of the Soviet armed forces during World War II, the PPSh-41 was a durable, low-maintenance weapon made of cheap, easily-obtained components. They utilise 7.62x25mm cartridge.\n \n
en.st_wpn_ppsh_bas_descr:
A major infantry weapon of the Soviet armed forces during World War II, the PPSh-41 was a durable, low-maintenance weapon made of cheap, easily-obtained components. They utilise 7.62x25mm cartridge.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Can be upgraded using Tactical Kit.
  •  Scopes: None 
  •  Suppressor: None
en.st_wpn_ppsh_bas_uptacppsh_descr:
A major infantry weapon of the Soviet armed forces during World War II, the PPSh-41 was a durable, low-maintenance weapon made of cheap, easily-obtained components. They utilise 7.62x25mm cartridge.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  The tactical kit "Bandolero" is installed on the weapon, which includes the EKP-8-18 collimator sight and improved barrel casing (heat is removed faster), as a result of which it became possible to increase the rate of fire compared to the original. (This upgrade is irreversible).
  •  Scopes: Axion Kobra EKP-8-18 reflex sight - included.
  •  Suppressor: None
en.st_wpn_protecta_aim_descr:
The uniquely designed Armsel Protecta is a rotating cylinder fed 12-gauge shotgun developed for riot control and combat. Antiquated now by the Russian Saiga-12 and the American AA-12, the Protecta was a revolutionary weapon created by the Apartheid government in the 1980s. This smoothbore combat revolver-type shotgun was intended to provide extreme firepower for militarised units. The weapon benefits from a large magazine and a high effective rate of fire, but its bulkiness and considerable weight, combined with a long reload time, are the price to be paid for these advantages. Rare in the Zone and used primarily to clear confined spaces of mutants. Specifically this variant is highly modernised, details inside are tightly fit to each other, muzzle energy is increased significantly thanks to higher pressure in the gun barrel. Sacrificing rate of fire, stopping power is increased. This model has Aimpoint AB collimator sight made by Swedish company.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  "Aimpoint" sight (integrated)
en.st_wpn_protecta_descr: The uniquely designed Armsel Protecta is a rotating cylinder fed 12-gauge shotgun developed for riot control and combat. Antiquated now by the Russian Saiga-12 and the American AA-12, the Protecta was a revolutionary weapon created by the Apartheid government in the 1980s. This smoothbore combat revolver-type shotgun was intended to provide extreme firepower for militarised units. The weapon benefits from a large magazine and a high effective rate of fire, but its bulkiness and considerable weight, combined with a long reload time, are the price to be paid for these advantages. Rare in the Zone and used primarily to clear confined spaces of mutants.
en.st_wpn_protecta_nimble_descr: The uniquely designed Armsel Protecta is a rotating cylinder fed 12-gauge shotgun developed for riot control and combat. Antiquated now by the Russian Saiga-12 and the American AA-12, the Protecta was a revolutionary weapon created by the Apartheid government in the 1980s. This smoothbore combat revolver-type shotgun was intended to provide extreme firepower for militarised units. The weapon benefits from a large magazine and a high effective rate of fire, but its bulkiness and considerable weight, combined with a long reload time, are the price to be paid for these advantages. Rare in the Zone and used primarily to clear confined spaces of mutants. According to certain sources, this model once belonged to Tank, but little is known about him.
en.st_wpn_rem700_aics_descr:
Model 700 rifles have been produced in the USA by Remington since 1962. It is possibly the most widely used bolt-action sniper and hunting rifle in the United States. It is the top product of Remington Arms. There are many variants of the rifle for different cartridges, however, the .338 Lapua Magnum cartridge was chosen for ammunition for this model for firing at long and extra-long distances. The rifle is fitted with a AICS (Accuracy International Chassis Systems) stock, 22" PGW Defense C14 fluted barrel and muzzle brake compensator, as well as a Badger Ordanance Embedded Front Rail for additional night sights.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: Schmidt&Bender PM II 3-12x50 (included), PN-23 "Nozzle" 
  •  Suppressor: PGW Defense C14 Timberwolf.
en.st_wpn_rem700_mcmillan_descr:
Model 700 rifles have been produced in the USA by Remington since 1962. It is possibly the most widely used bolt-action sniper and hunting rifle in the United States. There are many variants of the rifle for different cartridges, however, the .338 Lapua Magnum cartridge was chosen for ammunition for this model for firing at long and extra-long distances. The rifle is fitted with a McMillan A5 stock, 26" PGW Defense C14 fluted barrel and muzzle brake compensator, as well as a Badger Ordanance Embedded Front Rail for additional night sights.
 ATTACHMENTS:

en.st_wpn_remington700_bas_archangel_descr:
The Remington Model 700 is a series of bolt-action centerfire rifles manufactured by Remington Arms since 1962. This model has Promag Archangel polymer stock installed on it.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Installed Promag Archangel polymer stock
  •  Daniel Defence Wave QD suppressor
en.st_wpn_remington700_bas_descr:
The Remington Model 700 is a series of bolt-action centerfire rifles manufactured by Remington Arms since 1962.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Can be upgraded using: MOD X Gen.3 chassis, Magpul "PRO 700", Promag Archangel polymer stock and The Deer Hunter .338 kit
  •  Daniel Defence Wave QD suppressor
en.st_wpn_remington700_bas_lapua700_descr:
The Deer Hunter is modification of Remington Model 700 for precise shooting on very long distances. 
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  The weapon was upgraded using The Deer Hunter .338 kit
  •  Silencer: M700 .338LM
en.st_wpn_remington700_bas_magpul_pro_descr:
The Remington Model 700 is a series of bolt-action centerfire rifles manufactured by Remington Arms since 1962. This model has Magpul PRO 700 chassis and additional Trijicon RMR Red Dot Sight installed on it.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Installed Magpul "PRO 700" chassis
  •  Daniel Defence Wave QD suppressor
en.st_wpn_remington700_bas_mod_x_gen3_descr:
The Remington Model 700 is a series of bolt-action centerfire rifles manufactured by Remington Arms since 1962. This model has MOD X GEN III Modular Rifle System and additional Leupold DeltaPoint PRO Red Dot Sight installed on it.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Installed MOD X Gen.3 chassis
  •  Daniel Defence Wave QD suppressor
en.st_wpn_remington700_descr:
There are many options for a different rifle cartridge. Standard cartridge - 7.62x51 mm NATO (.308 Win). Technically, it is a magazine rifle with a longitudinally sliding rotary bolt. Store capacity depends on the caliber and can be from 3 to 5. It is a top product from Remington Arms.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  KZRZP suppressor (after modification)
en.st_wpn_remington870_descr:
Remington 870 is an American gun, first introduced in 1950 by Remington Arms. Due to its characteristic recharge is one of the most famous pump-action rifles in the world. Produced in various versions, it is suitable for both the military and the police, for hunters and athletes, as well as for self-defense.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Barska ES AC10632, C-more Railway Tactical, EOTech scopes
en.st_wpn_rg6_descr: The RG-6 (index 6G30) was in development between 1993 and 1994 by the TSKIB SOO. The new stand-alone, rapid-firing multi-shot launcher was required to increase the firepower of the infantry during the urban combat, due to recent small scale conflicts, such as the Chechen Wars. The RG-6 entered limited production by mid-1990s and is now in use by various elements of the Russian Army and special forces, as well as by special forces in MVD (Internal Affairs Ministry). The RG-6 is designed to fire all standard 40mm caseless grenades, available for general issue underbarrel launchers, such as GP-25 and GP-30. These rounds include VOG-25 fragmentation grenade, VOG-25P bouncing fragmentation grenade and "Gvozd" (Nail) nonlethal tear gas grenade for riot control. The drum is also front-loaded. Widely used for rapid suppression of enemy firing positions and, when in the open, attacking defended fixed targets or fighting particularly tough mutants.
en.st_wpn_rp_descr: Fitted with a skeleton buttstock made from wood as well as a hinged buttplate and a wooden pistol grip. The rear part of the buttstock houses a cleaning kit, and a disassembled cleaning rod is stored in the right leg of the bipod. A very reliable machine gun.\n \n
en.st_wpn_rpd_descr: The RPD (Degtyaryov hand-held machine gun) is a light machine gun developed in the Soviet Union by Vasily Degtyaryov for the intermediate 7.62x39mm M43 cartridge.
en.st_wpn_rpg26_descr:
RPG-26 "Aglen" is a Soviet disposable anti-tank rocket launcher developed by NPO Basalt to replace the RPG-22 "Net", to increase the combat capabilities of motorized rifle, airborne and other units. In the RPG-26, unlike its predecessors, the possibility of a reverse transfer from a combat position to a marching position was implemented.
 
 ONE-TIME USE!
en.st_wpn_rpg7_descr:
The RPG-7 is a further development of the previous RPG-2 anti-tank grenade launcher. The RPG-7, in its first version, known as RPG-7V, has been adopted by the Soviet army in 1961, and is still fielded in Russia and at least 50 more countries. By far, RPG-7 can be considered as one of the most successful anti-tank grenade launchers ever made. Initially thought of as an anti-armour weapon, it was later adopted for other fire support missions with the introduction of other types of grenades, such as anti-infantry HE-FRAG and multi-purpose Thermobaric/FAE. The RPG-7 is a shoulder fired, single shot, smoothbore recoilless launcher, grenades are loaded from the front, and the rear of the barrel is fitted with venturi nozzle. Due to the recoilless design, the backblast is dangerous. The RPG-7 is usually issued with 2.7X fixed magnification optical sight, designated PGO-7. It is a simple and inexpensive, but possess a high threat to modern tanks and can also serve in a number of other roles, making it a flexible weapon.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: PGO-7V Sight
en.st_wpn_rpk74_16_descr:
Newest Russian light machine gun - RPK-16 chambered in 5.45x39mm rounds. Key features of this kind of weapon are - quick detachable barrels, enhanced ergonomics, weaver rails on a handguard and dust cover.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  All scopes for picatinny/weaver
  •  5.45/7.62x39 Hexagon Sound Suppressor
en.st_wpn_rpk74_descr:
The RPK (Ruchnoy Pulemyot Kalashnikova or Kalashnikov hand-held machine gun) is a 5.45x45mm light machine gun, developed by Mikhail Kalashnikov in the late 1950s. It was created as part of a program designed to standardise the small arms inventory of the Red Army, where it replaced the 7.62x39mm RPD light machine gun. Today, the RPK continues to be used by the armed forces of countries of the former Soviet Union and certain African and Asian nations. The Polish Army adopted the weapon in 1974, where it is used in airborne assault, reconnaissance and special operations units. The RPK was also manufactured under license in Bulgaria and Romania.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes 
  •  TGP-A suppressor
en.st_wpn_rpk_descr: The RPK is a Soviet light machine gun, developed by Mikhail Kalashnikov in 1961 based on the Kalashnikov assault rifle. Created as part of Soviet efforts to standardise the armoury of the Red Army, the RPK replaced the RPD and is still in use to present day.\n \n
en.st_wpn_saiga12s_descr:
The Saiga-12 is a Kalashnikov pattern, 12 gauge combat shotgun, manufactured by Izhmash. The Saiga-12 is recognizable by its Kalashnikov design with the large lever safety on the right side of the receiver, the optic mounting rail on the left and the large top mounted dust cover held in place by the rear of the recoil spring assembly. Earning respect in the Zone for its lethality, it is intended for engaging targets at short range (50 metres or less) or in confined spaces.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
  •  PBS-1
en.st_wpn_saiga12s_isg_descr:
A model developed by Kalashnikov USA on the basis of the Komrad 12 semi-automatic rifle for hunting and protecting members of ISG research teams from mutants. The weapon has both a dovetail mount and a picatinny bar for use with various types of sights. This model has a new type of trigger mechanism , which allows you to two rounds burst.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All Red Dot scopes for picatinny/weaver. 
  •  Silencer: SilencerCo Salvo® 12
en.st_wpn_saiga12s_m1_descr:
Nerd - is the evidence of the professor’s concern for his wards. This Saiga version was purchased specifically for personnel. Used in "negotiations" with especially dangerous mutants about the transfer of their organs as a gift to science .. Experienced hunters look with considerable envy at the "nerds" armed with this model.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All Red Dot scopes for Dovetail mount.
  •  Silencer: Hexagon 12K
en.st_wpn_saiga12s_m2_descr:
Saiga modified by mercs request. Picatinny rail and a massive muzzle brake compensator installed, significantly reducing recoil.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All Red Dot scopes for picatinny/weaver and for Dovetail mount except of OKP-7, Kobra EKP-1S-03, Kobra EKP-8-02.
  •  Silencer: SilencerCo Salvo® 12
en.st_wpn_scar_descr:
The Special Operations Forces Combat Assault Rifle (SCAR) is a modular rifle made by FN Herstal for SOCOM to satisfy the requirements of the SCAR competition. This family of rifle consist of two types, the SCAR-H and SCAR-L. The SCAR-H (Heavy) is chambered in 7.62x51mm NATO cartridge.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, EOTech scopes
  •  M203 grenade launcher
en.st_wpn_scar_l_tac_descr:
The FN SCAR-L (Special Operation Forces Combat Assault Rifle - Light) assault rifle chambered in 5.56x45 NATO rounds, was adopted by USSOCOM (United States Special Operations Command) as the Mk 16. Features a side-folding polymer stock and a free-floating, cold hammer-forged Mil-Spec barrel with hardchromed bore. Fully-ambidextrous operating controls instantly adapt the SCAR to any user or any shooting position. The receiver-integrated optical rail plus three accessory rails enable mounting of a wide variety of scopes, electronic sights, tactical lights and lasers. Manufactured by Fabrique Nationale Herstal. Comes in black and flat dark earth.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for picatinny/weaver, B.E. MEYERS MAWL-C1 tactical green laser aim modul (included) 
  •  Silencer: ADVANCED ARMAMENT (AAC) M4-2000

  •  Grenade Launcher: FN HERSTAL FN40GL Mk2

en.st_wpn_shak12_descr:
The newly adopted Close Quarters Battle rifle made by Izhmash. Developed in response to a growing need made apparent by the FSB, the ShAK-12 was purpose built to house an enormous stopping power ability inside of a compact rifle package. It borrows its 12.7x55mm cartridge from the VSSK Vykhlop sniper rifle and utilizes 20 round magazines. Along with sporting a full array of picatinny attachment rails, it also features a removable carry handle for larger optic mounting options. The short recoil operation of the barrel behaves much in the same way as a Barrett M82 does with the barrel recoiling with the bolt to assist in controllability and cartridge extraction. A weapon like this is sure to make it into any STALKER's wishlist, but few will know what it feels like to own it. Whatever the case, there is no suit in the Zone that can withstand the power this gun deals out. Just make sure you aim well, are close and have a good stash of money, because the ammunition is anything but "cheap and available."
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, C-more Railway Tactical, EOTech scopes
  •  12.7x55mm compatible suppressor
en.st_wpn_sig220_descr:
The SIG-Sauer P220, manufactured in the late 1970s by J.P. Sauer & Son Company, is often considered the best out of the box .45 calibre double action pistol. An instant success, the P220 was adopted by the Swiss and Japanese SDF and by many European police organisations. This pistol also found its way into the US, first through the Browning Arms Company of Utah and since the 1980s, through the SIGARMS Company of New Hampshire.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  .45 ACP Suppressor

This pistol also found its way into the US, first through the Browning Arms Company of Utah and since the 1980s, through the SIGARMS Company of New Hampshire.

  •  Silencer: BLACK BOX .45
en.st_wpn_sig220_n_upg220_descr:

An instant success, the P220 was adopted by the Swiss and Japanese SDF and by many European police organisations. This pistol also found its way into the US, first through the Browning Arms Company of Utah and since the 1980s, through the SIGARMS Company of New Hampshire.

  •  Silencer: BLACK BOX .45
en.st_wpn_sig220_nimble_descr:
A unique model of the SIG-Sauer P220 handgun. Part of a small limited edition made for an unidentified special forces unit. The model benefits from an increased magazine capacity, a high durability steel barrel and an ultra-durable polymer receiver.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  .45 ACP Suppressor
en.st_wpn_sig226_226sig_kit_descr:
The SIG Sauer P226R is a full-sized, service-type pistol made by SIG Sauer, a standard service weapon of Navy SEALs. Chambered for the 9x19mm Parabellum, features a Picatinny rail mount on the underside of a frame.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Installed Tactical Kit: Threaded barrel for using with suppressor, ergonomic gripp plates and Trijicon RMR Red Dot sight with mounting plate. This upgrade is irreversible.
  •  Silencer: Osprey 9mm
en.st_wpn_sig226_descr:
The SIG Sauer P226R is a full-sized, service-type pistol made by SIG Sauer, a standard service weapon of Navy SEALs. Chambered for the 9x19mm Parabellum, features a Picatinny rail mount on the underside of a frame.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Can be upgraded using Tactical Kit.
  •  Silencer: none
en.st_wpn_sig550_descr:
Renowned for its high production quality and good performance, the SG 550 is often referred to as the finest 5.56 mm rifle ever made. It has a side-folding skeletonised buttstock and a lightweight aluminium bipod that folds into grooves in the lower handguard.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, EOTech scopes
  •  M203 grenade launcher
en.st_wpn_sig550_siber_descr:
Renowned for its high production quality and good performance, the SG 550 is often referred to as the finest 5.56 mm rifle ever made. It has a side-folding skeletonised buttstock and a lightweight aluminium bipod that folds into grooves in the lower handguard.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All for picatinny/weaver (except of Red Dot Sights with low mount: Trijicon RMR, Leupold DeltaPoint PRO, Aimpoint T1 (low mount), Docter Sight and 1P76 Rakurs (picatinny).
  •  Suppressor: ADVANCED ARMAMENT (AAC) M4-2000
en.st_wpn_sig550_sniper_descr:
A marksman variant of a popular Swiss-made assault rifle, the SG 550-S constitutes a perfect middle ground between sniper and assault rifles, retaining the strong points of both types of weaponry. On the other hand, it suffers from certain drawbacks such as being somewhat lacking in stopping power and accuracy for a sniper rifle and lack of automatic fire. Still, it remains one of the best top-tier weapon systems available within the Exclusion Zone.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
  •  Kahles ZF95 scope (integrated)
en.st_wpn_sig550_strelok_descr:
A Swiss made SIG SG-550 assault rifle. Although it isn't new, it is clear that the previous owner took good care of it: the weapon has been taken apart and put back together with every component fine-tuned for optimum performance, increasing stopping power. The letter "S" has been carved into the stock, along with 16 small notches.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, EOTech scopes
  •  M203 grenade launcher
en.st_wpn_sig550_tac_siber_descr:
Renowned for its high production quality and good performance, the SG 550 is often referred to as the finest 5.56 mm rifle ever made. It has a side-folding skeletonised buttstock and a lightweight aluminium bipod that folds into grooves in the lower handguard.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Installed Upgrades: Tactical handguard, Vertical grip, AN/PEQ-16 Laser Aim modul and Tritium sights.
  •  Scopes: All for picatinny/weaver (except of Red Dot Sights with low mount: Trijicon RMR, Leupold DeltaPoint PRO, Aimpoint T1 (low mount), Docter Sight and 1P76 Rakurs (picatinny).
  •  Suppressor: ADVANCED ARMAMENT (AAC) M4-2000
  •  M203 grenade launcher
en.st_wpn_sig552_descr:
The SG 552 Commando is designed for urban combat, making it the most compact member of SIG's renowned SG 550 family, which enhances its CQC capabilities.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, EOTech scopes
en.st_wpn_sig552_isg_siber_descr:
The SG 552 Commando is designed for urban combat, making it the most compact member of SIG's renowned SG 550 family, which enhances its CQC capabilities. This version of SG552 was seen in use of ISG infiltration teams.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Installed Upgrades: Magpul tactical grip and sights, B.E. MEYERS MAWL-C1 tactical green laser aim modul.
  •  Scopes: All for picatinny/weaver (except of Red Dot Sights with low mount: Trijicon RMR, Leupold DeltaPoint PRO, Aimpoint T1 (low mount), Docter Sight and 1P76 Rakurs (picatinny).
  •  Suppressor: Rifle Dynamics ISG Sound Suppressor

en.st_wpn_sks_b_descr:
The SKS is one of the first weapons chambered for the 7.62x39mm round. Replaced in Soviet front-line service by the AK-47 in the early 1950s, it remained in second-line service for decades and is still used as a ceremonial arm today.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
•  none
en.st_wpn_sks_descr:
The SKS is one of the first weapons chambered for the 7.62x39mm round. Replaced in Soviet front-line service by the AK-47 in the early 1950s, it remained in second-line service for decades and is still used as a ceremonial arm today.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
•  PU scope
en.st_wpn_sks_molot_descr:
Molot OP-SKS Carbine is designed for loading with 7.62x39 - the very popular ammunition in Zone. The weight and dimensions make the carbine ergonomic for shooters of any age and height.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: all scopes for Dovetail mount
  •  Silencer: None
en.st_wpn_sks_tac_descr:
Molot OP-SKS Carbine is designed for loading with 7.62x39 - the very popular ammunition in Zone. The weight and dimensions make the carbine ergonomic for shooters of any age and height. The tactical option differs from the original in the presence of a mount for installing sights in the fore-end area and also the possibility of installing a silence. Also new light trigger was installed on this model that significantly increased the rate of fire
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  SKS SOCOM Rail: Integrated 
  •  Scopes: all scopes for Dovetail mount and all red dot sights (except of Aimpoint PRO and Aimpoint T1 with high mount)
  •  Silencer: Hexagon SKS 7.62x39 sound suppressor
en.st_wpn_spareparts_descr:
Spare parts for what looks to be an AKM assault rifle. The wooden parts are not in a very good condition, and the same can be said of metal elements. Only novices would be interested in buying them. However, it seems that they may be useful for equipment repairs. Certain Zoners replace some parts when finding a new weapon out in the open in order to avoid being hunted for stealing from someone's stash.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  military item
en.st_wpn_spas12_descr: The SPAS-12 is a dual-mode shotgun, designed for combat and anti-riot measures. It is employed by many special military and police units around the world. With its distinctive heat shield and folding stock, the SPAS-12 is an iconic firearm. Notable for its reliability and tactical flexibility. Despite its heavy weight, complex mechanism and considerable cost, it is in demand in the Zone due to its effectiveness against mutants.\n \n
en.st_wpn_spas12_nimble_descr: An army shotgun designed specifically for assault units and created on the basis of the Franchi SPAS-12. Capable of automatic fire like its predecessor, but provides better recoil compensation thanks to a balanced shock free system. Capable of automatic fire like its predecessor, but provides better recoil compensation thanks to a balanced shock free system.\n \n
en.st_wpn_sr1m_descr:
The Serdyukov semi-automatic pistol - also known as SPS and Gyurza (“Viper”), GRAU Index 6P53. Pistol designed for the Russian Federation special forces purposes by P. Serdyukov and I. Belyaev, for 9x21 mm cartridge.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Can be upgraded using Tactical Kit.
  •  Silencer: None
en.st_wpn_sr1m_sr1upgr1_descr:
The Serdyukov semi-automatic pistol - also known as SPS and Gyurza (“Viper”), GRAU Index 6P53. Pistol designed for the Russian Federation special forces purposes by P. Serdyukov and I. Belyaev, for 9x21 mm cartridge.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  The pistol was upgraded using tactical kit.
  •  Silencer: SR1MP silencer 9x21
en.st_wpn_sr25_descr:
The SR-25, Stoner Rifle, model 25 was developed by Reed Knight, owner of Knights Armament Co. and Eugene Stoner. The SR-25 is an AR-15 rifle scaled up to shoot 7.62x51mm NATO or .308 ammunition. 60% of parts in the rifle is interchangeable with AR-15 components. This rifle sold well among civilian shooters who needed an accurate semi-automatic rifle in 7.62x51mm NATO or .308 calibre for hunting or target shooting. This rifle also found a favour in US Military. All rifles built on the SR-25 platform share the same basic action, the AR-15/M16 system. Because of AR-15 style recoil buffer, which extends rearwards from the receiver, SR-25 cannot be fitted with a folding stock instead it is fitted with telescoping stock.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  SR-25 special suppressor
  •  Leupold Mark 4 scope (integrated)
  •  M203 grenade launcher
en.st_wpn_sr2_m1_descr:
SR.2 "Veresk" is a Russian submachine gun (manufactured by TsNIITOCHMASH), designed to engage enemy personnel in close combat (at a distance of up to 200 m) using individual body armor. The main advantage of the SR-2 is its high firepower, high accuracy of fire and the destructive effect of a bullet at close range. 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  All Red Dot sights for picatinny/weaver, and two scopes with magnification: PN-23 and Leupold Mark 8 CQBSS 1.1-8x24 . 
  •  Silencer: GZV-SR2
en.st_wpn_sr2_m2_descr:
SR.2 "Veresk" is a Russian submachine gun (manufactured by TsNIITOCHMASH), designed to engage enemy personnel in close combat (at a distance of up to 200 m) using individual body armor. The main advantage of the SR-2 is its high firepower, high accuracy of fire and the destructive effect of a bullet at close range.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Version of the SR-2M improved by the mechanic of Duty faction, Sergeant "Mangun" Gromov. The improvements included: a lightweight bolt group that increases the rate of fire, a Magpul stock for better ergonomics, a picatinny rail that allows you to mount red dot/magnification sights and an LA5 laser Aim Module (with a green beam).
  •  All for picatinny/weaver, except of MarchF, Leupold Mark 8, Susat and EOTS-62.
  •  Silencer: GZV-SR2
en.st_wpn_sr2_veresk_descr:
SR.2 "Veresk" is a Russian submachine gun (manufactured by TsNIITOCHMASH), designed to engage enemy personnel in close combat (at a distance of up to 200 m) using individual body armor. The main advantage of the SR-2 is its high firepower, high accuracy of fire and the destructive effect of a bullet at close range. 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: KP SR-2 Reflex Sight
  •  Can be upgraded using Tactical Kit.
  •  Silencer: GZV-SR2
en.st_wpn_sr2_veresk_sr2_upkit_descr:
SR.2 "Veresk" is a Russian submachine gun (manufactured by TsNIITOCHMASH), designed to engage enemy personnel in close combat (at a distance of up to 200 m) using individual body armor. The main advantage of the SR-2 is its high firepower, high accuracy of fire and the destructive effect of a bullet at close range. 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  The weapon was upgraded using tactical upgrade kit. (Irreversible upgrade) 
  •  Silencer: GZV-SR2
en.st_wpn_sv98_descr:
The SV-98 (Snaiperskaya Vintovka Model 1998) is a Russian bolt action sniper rifle designed by Vladimir Stronskiy. In 2003 special operations troops were armed with the 7.62 mm 6S11 sniper system comprising the SV-98 sniper rifle (index 6V10) and 7N14 sniper enhanced penetration round. The rifle has been used in combat during operations in Chechnya.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  PKS-07 scope (integrated)
en.st_wpn_svd_descr:
Originally intended for designated marksmen in every basic Soviet infantry unit, the Dragunov SVD was not designed for specialised sniper teams. Relatively light for a sniper rifle but well-balanced and having semiautomatic fire, its precision is good but not exceptional. It has an effective range of up to 800 metres.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
  •  KZRZP suppressor (after modification)
en.st_wpn_svd_m1_descr:
Originally intended for designated marksmen in every basic Soviet infantry unit, the Dragunov SVD was not designed for specialised sniper teams. Relatively light for a sniper rifle but well-balanced and having semiautomatic fire, its precision is good but not exceptional. It has an effective range of up to 800 metres.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All dovetail mount scopes except of except of red dot sights.
  •  Silencer: KZRZP suppressor (after modification)
en.st_wpn_svd_nimble_descr:
A hand-assembled version of the SVDm-2 sniper rifle with improved mechanisms for increased flatness. Little difference in appearance, but provides an ideal combination of accuracy and stopping power.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  1P59 "Hyperon" scope (integrated)
  •  KZRZP suppressor
en.st_wpn_svds_descr:
Dragunov sniper rifle with folding stock - the SVDS variant. Was specially designed for Russian paratroopers and special forces units.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All dovetail mount scopes except of except of red dot sights.
  •  Silencer: TGP-V Suppressor
en.st_wpn_svds_pmc_descr:
Dragunov sniper rifle with folding stock - the SVDS variant. Was specially designed for Russian paratroopers and special forces units. In the Zone similar models are usually used by mercenaries, Ukrainian special forces, various PMCs and other power structures for conducting special operations.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for picatinny/weaver except of red dot sights. 
  •  Silencer: Rotor 43 
  The kit includes:
  •  Rotor 43 thread adapter for SVD-S, 
  •  SAG MK1 Freefloat Chassis for SVD, 
  •  Zenit foregrip RK-5 (short) for very good recoil control
  •  Custom SVDS dust cover,
  •  Zenit RK3 pistol grip, 
  •  Universal mount by NcStar that allows installing the auxiliary sight at 45 degrees angle with Trijicon RMR red dot sight installed on it.
en.st_wpn_svt40_descr:
The SVT-40 is a surplus semi-automatic rifle chambered in the 7.62x54mmR round. It saw widespread use in the Red Army and Wehrmacht during WWII.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
•  PU special scope
en.st_wpn_svu_descr:
The OTs-03 SVU is a rare, compact version of the SVD sniper rifle, created for use by the airborne infantry, but later employed mainly by the Russian Ministry of Internal Affairs. The main difference between it and the SVD is it's compact size and thus improved handling, thanks to a bullpup configuration and shortened barrel, making it sought after by stalkers in the Zone.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
en.st_wpn_svu_nimble_descr:
The SVU-A is a variant of the SVU rifle capable of automatic fire, designed on a request from the Russian Interior Ministry. Although the weapon is generally not suited for close-combat situations, the automatic fire has proven itself to be a reliable emergency solution for exposed marksmen. This particular model of SVU-A used to belong to Max, the legendary guardian of Freedom.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
en.st_wpn_toz106_descr:
Single-barreled 20ga hunting shotgun with sliding breech bolt. Widely known by it vernacular name, the Death of the Chairman, for its compact size.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: None 
 
  •  Silencer: None
en.st_wpn_toz106_m1_descr:
Single-barreled 20ga hunting shotgun with sliding breech bolt. Widely known by it vernacular name, the Death of the Chairman, for its compact size.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: Due to the position of the stock, it is possible to install sights only with a very low mount: Barska AC10632, C-More, Docter Sight, Aimpoint T1, Trijicon RMR, Leupold DeltaPoint PRO, OKP-7, 1P76 Rakurs. 
 
  •  Silencer: None
en.st_wpn_toz194_descr: The TOZ-194 is a 12 Gauge, pump action shotgun manufactured by the Tula Arms Plant. Designed during the last years of the Soviet Union, the manufacture of this firearm has started after its collapse, and since then it has gained a certain popularity within civilian shooters in Russia and in Europe and is reported to be in use with some Russian Security forces.\n \n
en.st_wpn_toz34_bull_descr: Hand made modification of TOZ-34 that uses only specially made "Bull" ammunition that could probably stop a tank. Modifications are mostly related to the strength of the gun, and at a first glance, it looks simply like a decorated TOZ-34. But when you take a closer look, you can see that barrel is made from a unique metal, betraying its non-standard origin, and the stock is also made from some type of carbon fibre, inscribed with the letter "F". It's hard to tell who made it and how it ended up in the Zone... \n \n
en.st_wpn_toz34_descr: Intended for professional and amateur hunting the TOZ-34 is an over/under shotgun. Though lacking in capacity, only 2 rounds, it compares well with high-grade shotguns due to its high accuracy of hits. The TOZ 34 was designed by N. I. Korovyakov and V. P. Ochnev in 1965. Affordable in price, with high damage output and its ability to unload both rounds instantly make it a popular weapon among rookies on the outskirts of the Zone. Especially useful against mutants thanks to its accuracy and stopping power.\n \n
en.st_wpn_toz34_mark4_descr:
Intended for professional and amateur hunting the TOZ-34 is an over/under shotgun. Though lacking in capacity, only 2 rounds, it compares well with high-grade shotguns due to its high accuracy of hits. The TOZ 34 was designed by N. I. Korovyakov and V. P. Ochnev in 1965. Affordable in price, with high damage output and its ability to unload both rounds instantly make it a popular weapon among rookies on the outskirts of the Zone. Especially useful against mutants thanks to its accuracy and stopping power. This model has a Leupold Mark 4 scope attached to it.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Leupold Mark 4 scope (integrated)
en.st_wpn_toz34_obrez_descr: Intended for professional and amateur hunting the TOZ-34 is a over/under shotgun. Modifications made to this gun by removing much of the barrel and the stock makes this shotgun more compact and easier to carry. Popular among rookies and bandits as they can hide it easily in their trench coats.\n \n
en.st_wpn_trg_descr:
The TRG-42 is not an accurised version of an existing rifle, but was developed from the very beginning as a sniper rifle. Its consistent high accuracy and reliability have made it popular both in long-range competitions and military sniper operations.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  FINDOT scope (integrated)
en.st_wpn_type63_descr:
The Type 63, often incorrectly referred to as Type 68 by Western sources, is a Chinese-designed rifle with a resemblance to the SKS. However, the weapon uses a rotating bolt working system from the AK-47 rifle instead of the tilting bolt system of the SKS.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  PU scope
en.st_wpn_umirotvoritel_bas_descr: An unknown weapon, the firing mechanism of which has not been established. The weapon appears faulty - squeezing the trigger doesn't do anything. It's very heavy and definitely high-tech. Of note is that it appears not to fire bullets based on ignition. Most probably, the rounds are propelled by electro-magnetic forces. One can only suspect what kind of destructive potential it possesses.
en.st_wpn_ump45_descr:
The UMP was developed by H&K in the 1990s, first appearing on the market in 1999. The idea behind the UMP was to create lightweight and powerful submachine gun, that was also cheaper than the MP5. The UMP, being targeted primarily for US law enforcement, appeared in .45ACP and .40SW chamberings, and later in 9mm. Providing greater stopping power than the MP5, the UMP45 also produces more recoil. This makes controlling full automatic fire more difficult. The UMP45's cyclic rate of fire was lowered significantly to combat this problem.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, EOTech scopes
  •  Gemtech suppressor
en.st_wpn_usas12_descr: The Daewoo Precision Industries USAS-12 (Universal Sports Automatic Shotgun 12 gauge) is an automatic shotgun designed for combat manufactured in South Korea by Daewoo Precision Industries in 1980s. How a Korean gun made it into Ukraine is something stalkers would very much like to know. Maybe there's a K2 rifle out there.\n \n
en.st_wpn_usp_descr:
Originally designed for the American market, the USP, Universal Self loading Pistol, is known as an accurate and reliable handgun. U.S. law enforcement and military provided much of the design criteria for the USP. The controls are uniquely American, influenced by famous designs like the Government Model 1911 pistol. All USPs use a fibre-reinforced polymer frame stiffened by stainless steel inserts at areas subject to stress and friction. Incorporating several successful handgun design elements, the USP Compact .45 is a part of the USP family of handguns designed by H&K. Able to withstand extreme punishment and adverse conditions, it is used by several countries' police and special forces units.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  .45 ACP suppressor (after modification)
en.st_wpn_usp_match_descr:
An enhanced USP by HK with distinct characteristics that make it more attractive to the target shooter. With the O-Ring barrel first seen in the Mark 23, the Match includes compensator that helps reduce the relatively mild kick of the 9x19. The Match also features polygonal rifling, a trigger stop, and fully adjustable rear sight.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  GemTech suppressor (after modification)
en.st_wpn_usp_nimble_descr:
A compact, large calibre pistol with short bolt movement and lightweight bolt assembly. Used to belong to a mercenary called Wolfhound. Appears to be a modified USP Compact pistol.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  GemTech suppressor (after modification)
en.st_wpn_usp_tac_descr:
This is modification of the HK USP Match specially developed for scientists by the personal order of Professor Sakhorov, designed for use in the "Specialist-Scientist" kit. The operation of the system of mechanisms is improved with the help of artifacts. The difference from the standard USP Match is the ability to install a silencer. The gun is equipped with an enlarged magazine for 22 rounds of 9mm caliber. (The Specialist-Scientist kit includes: BCC Savant, HK USP Match Tactical, Monolithh Blade).
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  The kit includes Laser Aiming Module and Red Dot Sights mount.
  •  Scopes: Docter Sight, Aimpoint T1 (low mount), Trijicon RMR, Leupold DeltaPoint PRO.
  •  Silencer: AAC Black Box
en.st_wpn_val_descr:
The AS Val is widely used by Russian Army recon units, as well as by MVD (Internal Affairs Ministry) and FSB (Federal Security Bureau) Special Forces. The AS-VAL is a gas operated, integrally suppressed weapon. The receiver is machined from steel forging for improved strength. It has a standard side-mounted rail for optical, night vision or red dot scopes but has no provision for mounting a bayonet or a grenade launcher. The integral suppressor could be easily detached for maintenance, repair, or compact storage, but the rifle should not be fired with the suppressor removed due to safety and reliability issues.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1M2-1 scopes
  •  VSS suppressor (integrated)
en.st_wpn_val_modern_descr:
The AS Val is widely used by Russian Army recon units, as well as by MVD (Internal Affairs Ministry) and FSB (Federal Security Bureau) Special Forces. The AS-VAL is a gas operated, integrally suppressed weapon. The receiver is machined from steel forging for improved strength. It has a standard side-mounted rail for optical, night vision or red dot scopes but has no provision for mounting a bayonet or a grenade launcher. The integral suppressor could be easily detached for maintenance, repair, or compact storage, but the rifle should not be fired with the suppressor removed due to safety and reliability issues.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
  •  VSS suppressor (integrated)
en.st_wpn_val_tac_descr:
The AS Val is widely used by Russian Army recon units, as well as by MVD (Internal Affairs Ministry) and FSB (Federal Security Bureau) Special Forces. The AS-VAL is a gas operated, integrally suppressed weapon. The receiver is machined from steel forging for improved strength. It has a standard side-mounted rail for optical, night vision or red dot scopes but has no provision for mounting a bayonet or a grenade launcher. The integral suppressor could be easily detached for maintenance, repair, or compact storage, but the rifle should not be fired with the suppressor removed due to safety and reliability issues.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for dovetail mount
  •  VSS suppressor (integrated)
en.st_wpn_vepr12_descr:
The Vepr-12 is a multi-purpose semi-automatic shotgun, created by Molot-Oruzhie Ltd. It is patterned after Kalashnikov rifles, and built on the heavy RPK light machine gun receiver. Like the Kalashnikov rifle, it is a rotating bolt, gas operated gun that feeds from a 10 round magazine.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
  •  PBS-1 suppressor
en.st_wpn_vihr_descr:
The SR-3 "Vikhr" ("Whirlwind") compact assault rifle was developed in CNIITochMash and manufactured by Tula Arms Plant. Initially known as "MA" (Malogabaritnyj Avtomat - small size assault rifle), it was based on the suppressed 9x39mm AS-Val assault rifle, and intended for concealed carry by special VIP protection teams and State Security operatives, SR3M Vikhr is result of combining the qualities of SR-3, AS VAL and VSS Vintorez. SR-3M also has a larger capacity of 30 rounds, which also provides more reliable feeding during automatic fire.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1, PSO-1M2-1 scopes
  •  SR-3M suppressor
en.st_wpn_vintorez_1pn93_descr:
Designed at TsNIITochMash by Peter Serdyukov and his team, the new rifle was intended for various Spetsnaz troops of the Soviet Army, KGB and MVD. The VSS is quite popular among its users for its stealthy capabilities and the great lethality of the heavy 9x39mm bullets. The effective range of the VSS is limited to 300-400 metres. Standard sighting equipment consists of side rail on the receiver, which usually accommodates the 4x PSO-1M2-1 scope. Any other scope or night vision sight can be mounted on the appropriate mount. Backup iron sights consist of tangent rear and blade front. The rear sight is graduated for up to 400 metres.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  1PN93 "Tot" scope (integrated)
  •  VSS suppressor (integrated)
en.st_wpn_vintorez_descr:
Designed at TsNIITochMash by Peter Serdyukov and his team, the new rifle was intended for various Spetsnaz troops of the Soviet Army, KGB and MVD. The VSS is quite popular among its users for its stealthy capabilities and the great lethality of the heavy 9x39mm bullets. The effective range of the VSS is limited to 300-400 metres. Standard sighting equipment consists of side rail on the receiver, which usually accommodates the 4x PSO-1M2-1 scope. Any other scope or night vision sight can be mounted on the appropriate mount. Backup iron sights consist of tangent rear and blade front. The rear sight is graduated for up to 400 metres.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1M2-1 scopes
  •  VSS suppressor (integrated)
en.st_wpn_vintorez_isg_descr:
The brainchild of the founder of Rifle Dynamics, Jim Fuller. The model is developed on the basis of the well-known and well-established VSS Vintorez. One of the main differences from the original is the presence of a special mechanism that allows you to fire with a misfire of two bullets and a picatinny rail installed along the entire length of the weapon. Used by ISG special units.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ATPIAL (Advanced Target Pointer Illuminator Aiming Laser) AN/PEQ-15 produced by L3 Insight Technologies. Tactical device that combines laser designators in both visible and IR band with IR searchlight. 
  •  Universal mount by NcStar with Trijicon RMR red dot sight installed on it. 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for picatinny/weaver and dawtail except of 1P78 Kashtan.
  •  Some rumors say that this gun can be used with underbarrel grenade launcher) 
  •  Silencer: Integrated
en.st_wpn_vintorez_m1_descr:
This is early prototype of VSS Savant. This build is more popular between pro-stalkers because of lower price than final version. 
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All except of EOTS-62 and Tactical Kits (two scopes)
  •  supressor VSS (integrated)
en.st_wpn_vintorez_m2_descr:
At the request of the Syndicate of Mercenaries, a team of enthusiastic scientists led by Professor Sakhorov was engaged in finalizing the prototype. To develop and conduct field trials, it took about 20 artifacts from gravitational anomalies. As a result, this allowed the Picatini bar to be installed on the receiver without loss of accuracy, as well as improved mechanics, which led to higher reliability indicators..
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ATPIAL (Advanced Target Pointer Illuminator Aiming Laser) AN/PEQ-15 produced by L3 Insight Technologies. Tactical device that combines laser designators in both visible and IR band with IR searchlight. 
  •  Scopes: All except of 1P59 Giperon
  •  Fab Defense stock and foregrip are installed
  •  supressor VSS (integrated)
en.st_wpn_vintorez_n1_descr:
Designed at TsNIITochMash by Peter Serdyukov and his team, the new rifle was intended for various Spetsnaz troops of the Soviet Army, KGB and MVD. The VSS is quite popular among its users for its stealthy capabilities and the great lethality of the heavy 9x39mm bullets. The effective range of the VSS is limited to 300-400 metres. Standard sighting equipment consists of side rail on the receiver, which usually accommodates the 4x PSO-1M2-1 scope. Any other scope or night vision sight can be mounted on the appropriate mount. Backup iron sights consist of tangent rear and blade front. The rear sight is graduated for up to 400 metres.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for dovetail mount
  •  VSS suppressor (integrated)
en.st_wpn_vintorez_nimble_descr:
A special variant of the famous VSS sniper rifle. This model has been extensively customised. Its internal mechanism has been replaced with a better one, which results in increased muzzle velocity and accuracy. It has an effective range of up to 600 metres.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  PSO-1 scope (integrated)
  •  VSS suppressor (integrated)
en.st_wpn_vityaz_descr:
A variant of PP-19 with 30-round magazine chambered for 9x19mm, designed in 2004. It is used by patrol groups, Russian traffic police, Russian security police, and by units of the MVD.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, EOTech scopes
  •  PBS-1 suppressor
en.st_wpn_vsk94_descr:
The VSK-94 is a 9x39mm suppressed sniper rifle developed by the Russian KBP Instrument Design Bureau as a low-cost alternative to the VSS rifle. The rifle is based on the 9A-91 carbine. The VSK-94 was designed to accurately engage unarmoured targets at ranges up to 400m.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1M2-1 scopes
  •  9A-91/VSK-94 suppressor (integrated)
en.st_wpn_vssk_descr:
The VSSK is a Russian bullpup, straight-pull bolt-action sniper rifle chambered for the 12.7x55mm subsonic round. The weapon is also known by its other name, Vykhlop. It was developed in 2002 for special forces units of FSB.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  PKS-07 scope (integrated)
en.st_wpn_vz61_descr:
Originally chambered for .32 ACP when adopted by the Czechoslovak Army in 1961, mass production of the Skorpion vz. 68 never took place. Even so, a large number of pre-production vz. 68 models made their way into the Zone. 
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  9x19 compatible suppressor (after modification)
en.st_wpn_vz61_scorpion_descr:
The Skorpion vz. 61 (or Sa vz. 61 Skorpion) is a Czechoslovak machine pistol developed in 1959 by Miroslav Rybar (1924–1970) and produced under the official designation Samopal vzor 61 ("submachine gun model 1961") by the Ceska zbrojovka arms factory in Uhersky Brod from 1963 to 1979. Although it was developed for use with security forces, the weapon was also accepted into service with the Czechoslovak Army as a personal sidearm for lower-ranking army staff, vehicle drivers, armoured vehicle personnel and special forces. Currently the weapon is in use with the armed forces of several countries as a sidearm. Originally chambered for .32 ACP but most of the models made their way into the Zone is vz.65, vz.82 chambered for 9x18mm Makarov.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Suppressor: Scorpion supressor
en.st_wpn_vz61_scorpion_m1_descr:
The Skorpion vz. 61 (or Sa vz. 61 Skorpion) is a Czechoslovak machine pistol developed in 1959 by Miroslav Rybar (1924–1970) and produced under the official designation Samopal vzor 61 ("submachine gun model 1961") by the Ceska zbrojovka arms factory in Uhersky Brod from 1963 to 1979. Although it was developed for use with security forces, the weapon was also accepted into service with the Czechoslovak Army as a personal sidearm for lower-ranking army staff, vehicle drivers, armoured vehicle personnel and special forces. Currently the weapon is in use with the armed forces of several countries as a sidearm. Originally chambered for .32 ACP but most of the models made their way into the Zone is vz.65, vz.82 chambered for 9x18mm Makarov.
 ATTACHMENTS:
